name: sus_climate_aggregate
title: Integration of Climate and Health Data

args.name: health_data
args.doc:
A `climasus_df` object produced by `sus_spatial_join()`.
  Must contain columns `date` (Date), `code_muni` (character), and geometry
  column `geom`.

args.name: climate_data
args.doc:
A `climasus_df` object produced by `sus_climate_fill_inmet()`.
  Must contain `date` (Date or POSIXct), `station_code`, `latitude`, `longitude`,
  and climate variables.

args.name: climate_var
args.doc:
Character vector with climate variables to aggregate.
  Use `"all"` (default) to include all available variables.

args.name: time_unit
args.doc:
Temporal aggregation unit for raw climate data before join.
  Options: `"day"` (default), `"week"`, `"month"`, `"quarter"`, `"year"`,
  `"season"`. Relevant only when input data are hourly resolution.

args.name: temporal_strategy
climate_region
args.doc:
Character. Climate classification for parameter adaptation.
  Options: `"auto"` (default, auto-detect by latitude), `"tropical"` (North, Northeast),
  `"subtropical"` (Center-West, Southeast), `"temperate"` (South).

args.name: window_days
args.doc:
Integer. Number of days in window for `moving_window`,
  `offset_window`, `degree_days`, `threshold_exceedance`, `cold_wave_exceedance`,
  or `weighted_window`. Required for these strategies.

args.name: lag_days
args.doc:
Integer vector. Specific lags for `discrete_lag` or maximum lag
  for `distributed_lag`. Required for these strategies.

args.name: offset_days
args.doc:
Integer vector of length 2 for `offset_window`.
  Defines historical interval: `c(W1, W2)` aggregates from t-W2 to t-W1.

args.name: temp_base
args.doc:
Numeric. Temperature base for `degree_days` calculation.
  If `NULL` (default), uses region-specific default: 20degC (tropical), 18degC (subtropical),
  15degC (temperate). Use 11 degC for *Aedes aegypti* development, 10degC for *Plasmodium*.

args.name: gdd_temp_var
args.doc:
Character. Temperature column for `degree_days`.
  Default: `"tair_dry_bulb_c"`.

args.name: min_obs
args.doc:
Numeric (0 to 1). Minimum proportion of valid observations required
  within window. Default: 0.7 (70%).

args.name: threshold_value
args.doc:
Numeric. Threshold for `threshold_exceedance` or
  `cold_wave_exceedance`. If `NULL`, uses region-specific default.

args.name: threshold_direction
args.doc:
Character: `"above"` (default, > threshold) or
  `"below"` (< threshold). For `cold_wave_exceedance`, automatically set to `"below"`.

args.name: weights
args.doc:
Numeric vector (optional) for `weighted_window`. Defines weight
  for each day within window, from most recent (position 1) to oldest (position W+1).
  If `NULL`, linear decreasing weights are generated automatically.

args.name: lang
args.doc:
Language for messages: `"pt"` (Portuguese), `"en"` (English), or
  `"es"` (Spanish). Default: `"pt"`.

args.name: verbose
args.doc: Logical. If `TRUE`, displays progress messages. Default: `TRUE`.

name: sus_climate_anomaly
title: Climate Anomaly Computation vs. INMET Climatological Normals
description:
Computes climate anomalies by comparing observed meteorological data from
[sus_climate_inmet()] against 30-year climatological normals from
[sus_climate_normals()]. Three anomaly types are supported: absolute
(obs − normal), relative ((obs − normal) / |normal| × 100 %), and
standardized z-score ((obs − normal) / σ_obs). The result is a `climasus_df`
ready for epidemiological modelling with [sus_mod_dlnm()],
[sus_climate_compute_heatwaves()], or [sus_mod_vulnerability_index()].

args.name: observed
args.doc:
A `climasus_df` from [sus_climate_inmet()] or
  [sus_climate_aggregate()]. At minimum must contain: a station identifier
  column (see `station_col`), a date column (see `date_col`), and at least
  one climate variable column covered by the `vars` mapping.

args.name: normals
args.doc:
A `climasus_df` from [sus_climate_normals()]. Must contain
  columns `codigo`, `mes`, `decada`, `var_code`, and `valor`. Run
  [sus_climate_normals_meta()] to see available `var_code` values.

args.name: vars
args.doc:
Named character vector mapping **observed column names** to
  **normals `var_code` values**, e.g.
  `c(tair_max_c = "t_max", rainfall_mm = "precipitation")`.
  `NULL` (default) auto-detects variables using the built-in map for
  standard INMET columns.

args.name: method
args.doc:
Character. Anomaly computation method: `"absolute"` (default),
  `"relative"`, `"standardized"`, or `"all"`.

args.name: time_scale
args.doc:
Character. Temporal resolution for the anomaly:
  `"monthly"` (default) averages normals across the three decades of each
  month and aggregates observations by calendar month; `"decadal"` preserves
  the 10-day decade structure of INMET normals and is more precise for
  variables such as temperature extremes and precipitation.

args.name: station_col
args.doc:
Character. Name of the station identifier column in
  `observed`. Default `"station_code"`. Must contain INMET codes matching
  the `codigo` column in `normals`.

args.name: date_col
args.doc:
Character. Name of the date or datetime column in
  `observed`. Default `"date"`. Accepts `Date` or `POSIXct`.

args.name: lang
args.doc:
Character. Language for messages: `"pt"` (default), `"en"`,
  `"es"`.

args.name: verbose
args.doc: Logical. Print progress messages. Default `TRUE`.

name: sus_climate_compute_coldwaves
title: Detect coldwaves using multiple standard methodologies
description:
Applies seven coldwave detection methods (WHO, WMO, INMET, ECF, UTCI, WBGT,
HI) to a `climasus_df` at stage `"climate"` produced by
`sus_climate_inmet()`, `sus_climate_fill_gap()`, or
`sus_climate_compute_indicators()`. Aggregates hourly data to daily scale,
computes smoothed historical percentile thresholds from a reference baseline,
identifies coldwave days and discrete events per method, and returns a named
list with events, daily-flag, and annual-summary tables — all carrying
`sus_meta` at stage `"climate"` / type `"coldwaves"`.

args.name: df
args.doc:
A `climasus_df` at stage `"climate"` with type `"inmet"`,
  `"filled"`, or `"indicators"`. Produced by `sus_climate_inmet()`,
  `sus_climate_fill_gap()`, or `sus_climate_compute_indicators()`. Must
  contain columns `date` and `station_code`. Temperature columns
  `tair_max_c`, `tair_min_c`, `tair_dry_bulb_c` are required for WHO/WMO/
  INMET/ECF; `utci_c`, `wbgt_c`, `hi_c` are required for UTCI/W

args.name: method
args.doc:
Character vector. One or more of `"WHO"`, `"WMO"`, `"INMET"`,
  `"EHF"`, `"UTCI"`, `"WBGT"`, `"HI"`. Use `"all"` to run every method.
  Methods whose required columns are absent emit a warning and are skipped.
  Note: the internal algorithm for the `"EHF"` slot uses the Excess Cold
  Factor (ECF) formula.

args.name: baseline_start
args.doc:
Date or character (e.g., `"1981-01-01"`). Start of the
  reference period for percentile thresholds. `NULL` uses the full series.

args.name: baseline_end
args.doc:
Date or character. End of the reference period.
  `NULL` uses the full series.

args.name: percentile
args.doc:
Numeric (0-100, default `10`). Percentile used for WHO,
  WMO, UTCI, WBGT, and HI threshold calculations (lower tail for coldwaves).

args.name: min_duration
args.doc:
Integer or `NULL`. Minimum consecutive days to qualify as
  a coldwave. `NULL` uses method defaults: WHO=3, WMO=5, INMET=5, EHF=3,
  UTCI=3, WBGT=3, HI=3.

args.name: lang
args.doc: Character. Output language: `"pt"` (default), `"en"`, `"es"`.

args.name: verbose
args.doc: Logical. Print progress messages. Default `TRUE`.

name: sus_climate_compute_heatwaves
title: Detect heatwaves using multiple standard methodologies
description:
Applies seven heatwave detection methods (WHO, WMO, INMET, EHF, UTCI, WBGT,
HI) to a `climasus_df` at stage `"climate"` produced by
`sus_climate_inmet()`, `sus_climate_fill_gap()`, or
`sus_climate_compute_indicators()`. Aggregates hourly data to daily scale,
computes smoothed historical percentile thresholds from a reference baseline,
identifies heatwave days and discrete events per method, and returns a named
list with event, daily-flag, and annual-summary tables — all carrying
`sus_meta` at stage `"climate"` / type `"heatwaves"`.

args.name: df
args.doc:
A `climasus_df` at stage `"climate"` with type `"inmet"`,
  `"filled"`, or `"indicators"`. Produced by `sus_climate_inmet()`,
  `sus_climate_fill_gap()`, or `sus_climate_compute_indicators()`. Must
  contain columns `date` and `station_code`. Temperature columns
  `tair_max_c`, `tair_min_c`, `tair_dry_bulb_c` are required for WHO/WMO/
  INMET/EHF; `utci_c`, `wbgt_c`, `hi_c` are required for UTCI/W

args.name: method
args.doc:
Character vector. One or more of `"WHO"`, `"WMO"`, `"INMET"`,
  `"EHF"`, `"UTCI"`, `"WBGT"`, `"HI"`. Use `"all"` to run every method.
  Methods whose required columns are absent emit a warning and are skipped.

args.name: baseline_start
args.doc:
Date or character (e.g., `"1981-01-01"`). Start of the
  reference period for percentile thresholds. `NULL` uses the full series.

args.name: baseline_end
args.doc:
Date or character. End of the reference period.
  `NULL` uses the full series.

args.name: percentile
args.doc:
Numeric (0–100, default `90`). Percentile used for WHO,
  WMO, UTCI, WBGT, and HI threshold calculations.

args.name: min_duration
args.doc:
Integer or `NULL`. Minimum consecutive days to qualify as
  a heatwave. `NULL` uses method defaults: WHO=3, WMO=5, INMET=5, EHF=3,
  UTCI=3, WBGT=3, HI=3.

args.name: lang
args.doc: Character. Output language: `"pt"` (default), `"en"`, `"es"`.

args.name: verbose
args.doc: Logical. Print progress messages. Default `TRUE`.

name: sus_climate_compute_indicators
title: Compute Bioclimatic and Thermal Stress Indicators

args.name: df
args.doc: Data frame or tibble from `sus_climate_inmet()` or `sus_climate_fill_inmet()`.

args.name: indicators
args.doc: Character vector of indicator codes or `"all"` (default).

args.name: region
args.doc:
Character. `"auto"` (default, standard thresholds) or a
  Brazilian biome: `"amazon"`, `"cerrado"`, `"caatinga"`,
  `"atlantic_forest"`, `"pampa"`, `"pantanal"`, `"southeast"`, `"south"`.

args.name: datetime_col
args.doc: Character or `NULL` (auto-detected).

args.name: station_col
args.doc: Character or `NULL` (auto-detected).

args.name: apply_validity_mask
args.doc:
Logical (default `TRUE`). When `region=="none"`,
  masks HI, WCET, WCT outside their valid meteorological domains with `NA`.

args.name: confidence_flags
args.doc:
Logical. Add `_flag_extreme`, `_flag_high`,
  `_flag_low` columns. Default `TRUE` when region is set, `FALSE` otherwise.

args.name: custom_thresholds
args.doc: Named list to override regional defaults.

args.name: keep_source_vars
args.doc: Logical (default `FALSE`).

args.name: verify_physics
args.doc:
Logical (default `TRUE`). Check solar radiation
  against extraterrestrial limits; requires `latitude` column.

args.name: compute_uncertainty
args.doc: Logical (default `FALSE`). Monte Carlo 95% CI.

args.name: verbose
args.doc: Logical (default `TRUE`).

args.name: lang
args.doc: Character: `"pt"` (default), `"en"`, `"es"`.

args.name: use_cache
args.doc:
Logical. If `TRUE` (default), uses cached spatial data to avoid
  re-downloads and improve performance.

args.name: cache_dir
args.doc:
Character string specifying the directory to store cached files.
  Default is `"~/.climasus4r_cache/spatial"`.

name: sus_climate_compute_spei
title: Compute Standardized Precipitation-Evapotranspiration Index (SPEI)

args.name: df
args.doc:
A `climasus_df` at `stage = "climate"` with monthly data.
  Must contain `code_muni` (character), `date` (Date, first day of each
  month), and the precipitation column named by `rain_var`.

args.name: rain_var
args.doc:
Character. Name of the monthly precipitation column (mm).
  Default `"rainfall_chirps_mm"`. Other common value: `"rainfall_mm"`.

args.name: pet_var
args.doc:
Character. Name of the monthly potential evapotranspiration
  column (mm). Used when `pet_method = "column"`. Default `"pet_mm"`.
  Ignored when `pet_method = "thornthwaite"`.

args.name: pet_method
args.doc:
Character. How to obtain PET values. One of:
  \itemize{
    \item `"column"` (default): use the column named by `pet_var` directly.
    \item `"thornthwaite"`: compute Thornthwaite (1948) PET from monthly
      mean temperature in the column named by `temp_var`. Only needs
      temperature; less accurate than energy-balance methods but practical
      when only INMET or ERA5 T2m is available.

args.name: temp_var
args.doc:
Character. Name of the monthly mean temperature column (°C).
  Used only when `pet_method = "thornthwaite"`. Default `"tair_dry_bulb_c"`.

args.name: scales
args.doc:
Integer vector. SPEI timescales in months. Default
  `c(1L, 3L, 6L, 12L)`.

args.name: ref_start
args.doc:
Date or `NULL`. Start of the calibration period for
  log-logistic distribution fitting. `NULL` uses all available data.
  WMO recommends at least 30 years.

args.name: ref_end
args.doc:
Date or `NULL`. End of the calibration period. `NULL` uses
  all available data.

args.name: min_n
args.doc:
Integer. Minimum number of non-NA values required to fit the
  log-logistic distribution per location. Default `24L` (2 years).
  Locations with fewer values receive `NA`.

args.name: lang
args.doc: Character. Message language: `"pt"` (default), `"en"`, `"es"`.

args.name: verbose
args.doc: Logical. Print progress messages. Default `TRUE`.

name: sus_climate_compute_spi
title: Compute Standardized Precipitation Index (SPI)

args.name: df
args.doc:
A `climasus_df` at `stage = "climate"` with monthly precipitation.
  Must contain `code_muni` (character), `date` (Date, first day of each
  month), and the column named by `var`.

args.name: var
args.doc:
Character. Name of the monthly precipitation column.
  Default `"rainfall_chirps_mm"`. Other common value: `"rainfall_mm"`.

args.name: scales
args.doc:
Integer vector. SPI timescales in months. Default
  `c(1L, 3L, 6L, 12L)`. Common choices: 1 (soil moisture), 3 (short-term),
  6 (agriculture/water supply), 12 (groundwater/streamflow).

args.name: ref_start
args.doc:
Date or `NULL`. Start of the calibration period for
  gamma distribution fitting. `NULL` (default) uses all available data.
  WMO recommends at least 30 years; CHIRPS 1981–2010 is ideal.

args.name: ref_end
args.doc:
Date or `NULL`. End of the calibration period.
  `NULL` (default) uses all available data.

args.name: min_n
args.doc:
Integer. Minimum number of non-NA, non-zero precipitation
  values required to fit the gamma distribution per location. Default `24L`
  (2 years). Locations with fewer values receive `NA` for all SPI columns.

args.name: lang
args.doc: Character. Message language: `"pt"` (default), `"en"`, `"es"`.

args.name: verbose
args.doc: Logical. Print progress messages. Default `TRUE`.

name: sus_climate_inmet
title: Import and Process INMET Meteorological Data

args.name: years
args.doc:
Numeric vector of year(s) to import.
  Examples: `2020`, `2020:2024`, `c(2019, 2021, 2023)`.
  Must be between 2000 and current year. If `NULL`, imports last 2 years.

args.name: uf
args.doc:
Character vector of Brazilian state codes (e.g., `"AM"`,
  `c("RJ", "MG")`). Case insensitive. If `NULL` (default), imports
  **all 27 states** (may be slow - see **Performance**).

args.name: station_code
args.doc:
Character vector of INMET station codes to filter
  (e.g., `c("A101", "A122")`). Optional. If provided, the function downloads
  data for the requested `years`/`uf` and then filters to include only the
  specified stations. If `NULL` (default), all stations in the selected
  states are returned. Station codes are matched case-insensitively.

args.name: use_cache
cache_dir
args.doc:
Character. Directory path for disk cache.
  Default: `"~/.climasus4r_cache/climate"`. Created automatically.

args.name: parallel
workers
args.doc:
Integer. Number of parallel workers. Default: `4`.
  Ignored if `parallel = FALSE`. Uses `future::multisession` backend.
  Automatically capped at `availableCores() - 1`.

args.name: lang
verbose
args.doc:
Logical. If `TRUE` (default), prints detailed progress including:
  \itemize{
    \item Cache hits/misses
    \item Download progress with retry attempts
    \item QC modifications (rows corrected/removed)
    \item Final statistics
  }

name: sus_climate_normals
title: Download and Process INMET Climate Normals
description:
`sus_climate_normals()` retrieves 30-year climatological normals from the
Brazilian National Institute of Meteorology (INMET) for one of three
reference periods (1961-1990, 1981-2010, 1991-2020). It loads the variable
catalogue from the bundled `normal_meta.parquet` dictionary—with transparent
disk caching and automatic fallback download—then fetches each selected
variable's Excel file from the INMET portal, parses the decade-level monthly
structure, and returns a tidy long-format `climasus_df`.

args.name: period
args.doc:
Character. Reference climatological period. One of
  `"1961-1990"`, `"1981-2010"`, or `"1991-2020"` (default).

args.name: target_var
args.doc:
Character vector of `var_code` values to download
  (e.g. `c("t_max", "precipitation")`). `NULL` (default) downloads all
  variables available for the selected `period`. Run
  `sus_climate_normals_meta()` to inspect the catalogue.

args.name: cache_dir
args.doc:
Character. Local directory for disk-cached files.
  Default: `"~/.climasus4r_cache/normals"`.

args.name: use_cache
args.doc:
Logical. If `TRUE` (default), loads from and saves to
  `cache_dir`. Set to `FALSE` to force a fresh download.

args.name: lang
args.doc: Character. Message language: `"pt"` (default), `"en"`, `"es"`.

args.name: verbose
args.doc: Logical. Print progress messages. Default `TRUE`.

name: sus_climate_normals_meta
title: Browse the INMET Climate Normals Catalogue
description:
Returns the `normal_meta` dictionary as a tibble so you can inspect
available variables and their `var_code` values before calling
[sus_climate_normals()].

args.name: period
args.doc:
Character. Filter to a specific period, or `NULL` (default)
  to return the full catalogue.

args.name: lang
args.doc:
Character. Language for the returned label column:
  `"pt"` (default), `"en"`, `"es"`.

args.name: cache_dir
args.doc:
Character. Cache directory. Default:
  `"~/.climasus4r_cache/normals"`.

args.name: use_cache
args.doc: Logical. Use cached catalogue. Default `TRUE`.

args.name: verbose
args.doc: Logical. Print messages. Default `FALSE`.

name: sus_climate_plot_aggregate
title: Visualise Climate-Health Aggregate Data

args.name: df
args.doc:
A `climasus_df` at `stage = "climate"` produced by
  [sus_climate_aggregate()]. Must contain at least `date`, `code_muni`, a
  health outcome column, and one or more climate exposure columns.

args.name: outcome_col
args.doc:
Character. Name of the health-outcome column
  (e.g. `"n_obitos"`, `"n_internacoes"`). If `NULL` (default) the first
  integer column that is not a climate variable is used.

args.name: climate_cols
args.doc:
Character vector of climate column names to visualise.
  If `NULL` (default) all columns that match the naming conventions of
  [sus_climate_aggregate()] are detected automatically (`lag*`, `mvwin*`,
  `off*to*`, `gdd*`, `_lag*`, or known INMET variable names).

args.name: plot_type
args.doc:
Character. One of:
  \describe{
    \item{`"timeseries"`}{Dual-axis time-series: rescaled climate exposure
      (ribbon) overlaid on health outcome (line + points). When multiple
      climate columns are provided and `patchwork` is installed, one panel
      per variable is stacked vertically via `patchwork::wrap_plots()`. Falls
      back to the first column only when `patchwork` is not availab

args.name: smooth_method
args.doc:
Character. Smoothing method for the scatter plot:
  `"loess"` (default) or `"gam"`.

args.name: max_lag
args.doc:
Integer. Maximum lag (days) shown in the CCF plot.
  Default `30L`.

args.name: alpha
args.doc:
Numeric. Confidence level for CCF significance bounds
  (default `0.05`; bounds drawn at \eqn{\pm 2/\sqrt{n}}).

args.name: interactive
args.doc:
Logical. If `TRUE`, returns a `plotly` interactive
  version built with `plotly::plot_ly()` for proper dual-axis support
  (`plotly::ggplotly()` is avoided as it breaks secondary axes for
  `"timeseries"`). Requires `plotly` to be installed. Default `FALSE`.

args.name: lang
args.doc:
Character. Language for axis labels and titles:
  `"pt"` (default), `"en"`, `"es"`.

args.name: verbose
args.doc: Logical. Print progress messages. Default `TRUE`.

args.name: title
args.doc:
Character or `NULL`. Override the auto-generated plot title.
  Default `NULL` uses the built-in multilingual title.

args.name: source
args.doc:
Character or `NULL`. Data source attribution prepended to the
  plot caption. Default `NULL`.

name: sus_climate_plot_coldwaves
title: Plot and Analyze Coldwave Events

args.name: cw_result
args.doc: List. The output from `sus_climate_compute_coldwaves()`.

args.name: type
args.doc:
Character. Type of plot to generate:
  * `"timeline"`: Gantt-style timeline of coldwave events.
  * `"calendar"`: Calendar heatmap showing days with coldwaves.
  * `"intensity"`: Scatter plot of duration vs. coldest temperature.
  * `"trend"`: Bar chart of number of events per year.

args.name: station_code
args.doc: Character. Optional. Filter by a specific station code.

args.name: method
args.doc:
Character. Optional. Filter by a specific method (e.g.,
  `"EHF"`, `"INMET"`).

args.name: year
args.doc:
Numeric. Optional. Filter by a specific year (useful for
  calendar plots).

args.name: interactive
args.doc:
Logical. If `TRUE`, returns an interactive Plotly chart.
  If `FALSE`, returns a static ggplot2 chart. Default is `TRUE`.

args.name: color_palette
args.doc:
Character. Name of the ggsci palette to use. Default
  is `"npg"`. Blue tones are extracted for cold-event emphasis.

args.name: lang
args.doc:
Character. Language for labels: `"pt"` (default), `"en"`,
  `"es"`.

args.name: save_plot
args.doc:
Character. Optional file path to save the plot
  (e.g., `"plot.html"` or `"plot.png"`).

name: sus_climate_plot_fill
title: Visualise time-series gap-filling from sus_climate_fill()

args.name: df_filled
args.doc:
Output of `sus_climate_fill()`. In production mode this is a
  `<climasus_df>` tibble; in evaluation mode it is the named list returned by
  `sus_climate_fill(evaluation = TRUE)`.

args.name: df_original
args.doc:
Raw data before gap filling, e.g. from `sus_climate_inmet()`.
  Required in production mode; ignored in evaluation mode.

args.name: target_var
args.doc: Character vector of variable(s) to visualise.

args.name: interactive
args.doc: Logical. `TRUE` → plotly; `FALSE` → ggplot2 (default: `TRUE`).

args.name: output_type
args.doc:
What to return: `"plot"`, `"table"`, `"metrics"`, or `"all"`
  (default: `"all"`).

args.name: save_plot
args.doc:
Optional file path to save the plot (e.g. `"plot.png"` or
  `"plot.html"`).

args.name: lang
args.doc: Language: `"en"`, `"pt"`, or `"es"` (default: `"en"`).

args.name: color_palette
args.doc: ggsci palette name (default: `"npg"`).

args.name: verbose
args.doc: Print diagnostic information (default: `FALSE`).

name: sus_climate_plot_heatwaves
title: Plot and Analyze Heatwave Events

args.name: hw_result
args.doc: List. The output from `sus_climate_compute_heatwaves()`.

args.name: type
args.doc:
Character. Type of plot to generate:
  * `"timeline"`: Gantt-style timeline of heatwave events.
  * `"calendar"`: Calendar heatmap showing days with heatwaves.
  * `"intensity"`: Scatter plot of duration vs. intensity (EHF peak).
  * `"trend"`: Bar chart of number of events per year.

args.name: station_code
args.doc: Character. Optional. Filter by a specific station code.

args.name: method
args.doc: Character. Optional. Filter by a specific method (e.g., "EHF", "INMET").

args.name: year
args.doc: Numeric. Optional. Filter by a specific year (useful for calendar plots).

args.name: interactive
args.doc: Logical. If `TRUE`, returns an interactive Plotly chart. If `FALSE`, returns a static ggplot2 chart. Default is `TRUE`.

args.name: color_palette
args.doc: Character. Name of the ggsci color palette to use. Default is `"npg"`.

args.name: lang
args.doc: Character. Language for labels and titles: `"en"` (English), `"pt"` (Portuguese), or `"es"` (Spanish). Default is `"en"`.

args.name: save_plot
args.doc: Character. Optional file path to save the plot (e.g., "plot.html" or "plot.png").

name: sus_climate_uniplu
title: Import UNIPLU-BR: Unified Brazilian Rainfall Dataset

args.name: years
args.doc:
Integer vector of year(s) to import.
  Examples: `2020`, `2020:2024`, `c(2015, 2020, 2024)`.
  Must be between 1885 and the current year. If `NULL` (default), imports
  the last 2 years.

args.name: uf
args.doc:
Character vector of Brazilian state codes
  (e.g., `"RN"`, `c("SP", "RJ", "MG")`). Case insensitive. If `NULL`
  (default), all 27 states are returned.

args.name: network
args.doc:
Character vector of monitoring network(s) to include.
  One or more of: `"Hidroweb"`, `"INMET"`, `"ICEA"`, `"CEMADEN"`,
  `"Telemetria"`. Case insensitive. If `NULL` (default), all networks
  are returned.

args.name: aggregate_to
args.doc:
Character. Temporal resolution for aggregating sub-daily
  observations into totals. One of:
  \itemize{
    \item `"day"` (default): Daily rainfall totals (`sum(rain_mm)`)
    \item `"month"`: Monthly rainfall totals
    \item `"year"`: Annual rainfall totals
    \item `"none"`: Return raw observations at original resolution
  }
  When `"none"`, `rainfall_mm` reflects the raw measurement interval

args.name: use_cache
args.doc:
Logical. If `TRUE` (default), skips download when the two
  Parquet files already exist in `cache_dir/uniplu/`. The full ZIP (~1.6 GB)
  is only downloaded once. Use `use_cache = FALSE` to force a re-download
  (e.g., after a new dataset version is published on Zenodo).

args.name: cache_dir
args.doc:
Character. Directory path for the disk cache.
  Default: `"~/.climasus4r_cache/climate"`. Created automatically if absent.
  Use `unlink(file.path(cache_dir, "uniplu"), recursive = TRUE)` to clear
  the UNIPLU cache.

args.name: lang
verbose
args.doc:
Logical. If `TRUE` (default), prints progress messages
  including cache status, download progress, filtering counts, and
  aggregation summary.

name: sus_data_aggregate
title: Aggregate Health Data into Time Series
description:
Aggregates individual-level health data into time series counts by specified
time units and grouping variables. This function is essential for preparing
data for time series analysis, DLNM models, and other temporal epidemiological methods.

args.name: df
args.doc: A data frame containing health data (output from `sus_data_standardize()`, or `sus_data_filter*()`).

args.name: time_unit
args.doc:
Character string specifying the temporal aggregation unit.
  **Standard units**: `"day"`, `"week"`, `"month"`, `"quarter"`, `"year"`
  **Multi-day/week/month**: `"2 days"`, `"5 days"` (pentads), `"14 days"` (fortnightly),
   `"3 months"` (trimester), `"6 months"` (semester).
  **Special**: `"season"` (Brazilian seasons: DJF, MAM, JJA, SON).
  Default is `"day"`.

args.name: fun
args.doc:
Character string or list of functions specifying the aggregation function(s).
  Options: `"count"` (default), `"sum"`, `"mean"`, `"median"`, `"min"`, `"max"`,
  `"sd"`, `"q25"` (25th percentile), `"q75"`, `"q95"`, and `"q99"`.
  Can also be a named list for multiple aggregations, e.g.,
  `list(mean_temp = "mean", max_temp = "max")`.

args.name: group_by
args.doc:
Character vector with names of columns to group by (e.g.,
  `c("sex", "age_group", "race")`). If `NULL` (default), aggregates
  across `"municipality_code"` records.

args.name: value_col
args.doc:
Character string with the name of the column to aggregate when
  using functions other than `"count"`. Required for `"sum"`, `"mean"`, etc.
  For example, `"temperature"`, `"precipitation"`, `"pm25"`.

args.name: complete_dates
args.doc:
Logical. If `TRUE` (default), fills in missing time periods
  with zero counts to create a complete time series without gaps.

args.name: date_col
args.doc:
Character string with the name of the date column to use for
  aggregation. If `NULL` (default), the function will attempt to auto-detect
  the date column based on common patterns.

args.name: backend
args.doc:
Character string specifying the data processing backend.
  Use `"arrow"` for out-of-memory, lazy processing (recommended for large datasets),
  or `"tibble"` for in-memory processing (recommended for small to medium datasets).

args.name: lang
args.doc:
Character string specifying the language for messages. Options:
  `"en"` (English), `"pt"` (Portuguese, default), `"es"` (Spanish).

args.name: verbose
args.doc: Logical. If `TRUE` (default), prints progress messages.

name: sus_data_cid_select
title: Interactive Disease Groups Explorer
description:
Opens an interactive HTML interface to explore disease groups and climate factors
for use with `sus_data_filter_cid()`.

args.name: lang
args.doc:
Character string specifying language. Options: `"pt"` (Portuguese,
  default), `"en"` (English), `"es"` (Spanish).

args.name: output
filter_climate
args.doc: Logical. If `TRUE`, shows only climate-sensitive diseases.

args.name: verbose
args.doc: Logical. If `TRUE` (default), prints informative messages.

name: sus_data_clean_encoding
title: Detect and correct character encoding issues
description:
This function scans text columns in a data.frame and corrects common encoding
problems (e.g., "Sao Paulo") that occur when Latin1 data is
incorrectly read as UTF-8. It acts as a final auditor to ensure all text data
is properly encoded, complementing the preprocessing done by `microdatasus`.
Supports multilingual output messages (English, Portuguese, Spanish).

args.name: df
args.doc: A `data.frame` or `tibble` to be cleaned.

args.name: backend
args.doc:
Character string specifying the data processing backend.
  Use `"arrow"` for out-of-memory, lazy processing (recommended for large datasets),
  or `"tibble"` for in-memory processing (recommended for small to medium datasets).

args.name: lang
args.doc:
Character. Language for UI messages. Options: "en" (English), 
  "pt" (Portuguese, default), "es" (Spanish).

args.name: verbose
args.doc: Logical. If TRUE, prints a report of columns checked and corrected. Default is TRUE.

name: sus_data_create_variables
title: Create Derived Variables for Epidemiological Analysis
description:
Creates commonly used derived variables from health data across ALL SUS systems
(SIM, SINAN, SIH, SIA, SINASC, CNES), including age groups, calendar variables,
and other epidemiologically relevant categorizations. Features age
calculation that handles different data formats across systems.

args.name: df
args.doc: A data frame containing health data from any SUS system.

args.name: create_age_groups
args.doc:
Logical. If `TRUE`, derives age-based variables.
When enabled, the function will attempt to determine age using the following
hierarchy (in order of preference):
\enumerate{
  \item An existing age column supplied via \code{age_col}.
  \item Calculation from birth date and event date columns (gold standard).
  \item Decoding of DATASUS age code variables (fallback method).
}

args.name: age_breaks
args.doc:
Numeric vector specifying the breakpoints for age groups.
  Default is `c(0, 5, 15, 65, Inf)` for standard epidemiological categories.

args.name: age_labels
args.doc:
Character vector with labels for age groups. If `NULL`
  (default), generates labels automatically from breaks.

args.name: create_calendar_vars
args.doc:
Logical. If `TRUE`, creates calendar variables
  (day of week, month, season, etc.). Default is `FALSE`.

args.name: create_climate_vars
args.doc: Logical. Se `TRUE`, cria variaveis climaticas e sazonais.

args.name: climate_region
args.doc:
Character. Regiao climatica para calculos sazonais.
  Opcoes: "norte", "nordeste", "centro-oeste", "sudeste", "sul".

args.name: date_col
args.doc:
Character string with the name of the date column. If `NULL`
  (default), auto-detects the date column.

args.name: age_col
args.doc:
Character string with the name of the age column (in years).
  If `NULL` (default), auto-detects and calculates age using hierarchical logic:
  1. Direct age column (if exists)
  2. Calculate from dates (event_date - birth_date)
  3. Decode DATASUS age codes (NU_IDADE_N, IDADE)

args.name: hemisphere
args.doc:
Character string specifying the hemisphere for season
  calculation. Options: `"south"` (default, for Brazil), `"north"`.

args.name: backend
args.doc:
Character string specifying the data processing backend.
  Use `"arrow"` for out-of-memory, lazy processing (recommended for large datasets),
  or `"tibble"` for in-memory processing (recommended for small to medium datasets).

args.name: lang
args.doc:
Character string specifying the language for variable labels and
  messages. Options: `"en"` (English), `"pt"` (Portuguese, default), `"es"` (Spanish).

args.name: verbose
args.doc: Logical. If `TRUE` (default), prints progress messages.

name: sus_data_export
title: Export Processed Health Data with Metadata
description:
Exports processed health data to a file with optional metadata documentation
to ensure reproducibility. Supports multiple file formats optimized for
different use cases, including GeoArrow/GeoParquet for spatial data.

args.name: df
args.doc: A data frame or sf object containing the processed health data to export.

args.name: file_path
args.doc:
Character string specifying the output file path. The file
  extension determines the format if `format` is not explicitly specified.

args.name: format
args.doc:
Character string specifying the output format. Options:
  `"rds"` (default, R binary format), `"arrow"` (Apache Arrow/Parquet),
  `"geoparquet"` (GeoArrow/GeoParquet for spatial data), `"shapefile"` (ESRI Shapefile), `"GeoPackage"`, 
  and `"csv"` (comma-separated values).
  If `NULL`, infers from `file_path` extension and data type (auto-detects sf objects).

args.name: include_metadata
args.doc:
Logical. If `TRUE` (default), saves a companion
  metadata file (`.txt` or `.json`) with processing information.

args.name: metadata
args.doc:
Named list containing custom metadata to save. Common fields:
  \itemize{
    \item `source_system`: Health system (e.g., "SIM-DO")
    \item `states`: Vector of state codes
    \item `years`: Vector of years
    \item `filters_applied`: Description of filters
    \item `disease_groups`: Disease groups included
    \item `processing_date`: Date of processing
    \item `package_version`: climasus4r

args.name: compress
args.doc:
Logical. If `TRUE` (default for RDS and Arrow), compresses
  the output file. Compression level can be specified for some formats.

args.name: compression_level
args.doc:
Integer specifying compression level (1-9). Higher
  values = smaller files but slower. Default is 6. Only applies to formats
  that support compression.

args.name: overwrite
args.doc:
Logical. If `TRUE`, overwrites existing files. If `FALSE`
  (default), stops with an error if file exists.

args.name: lang
args.doc:
Character string specifying the language for messages. Options:
  `"en"` (English, default), `"pt"` (Portuguese), `"es"` (Spanish).

args.name: verbose
args.doc: Logical. If `TRUE` (default), prints export summary.

name: sus_data_filter_cid
title: Filter SUS health data by ICD-10 codes or disease groups with multilingual support
description:
Filters Brazilian Unified Health System (SUS) data based on ICD-10 codes.
(International Classification of Diseases, 10th Revision) or predefined epidemiological disease groups. This function
supports complex filtering scenarios including specific codes, code ranges,
chapters, and 50+ disease groups relevant to epidemiological research in Brazil.
Includes specialized support for SUS-specific coding practices and
multilingual interface (English, Portuguese, Spanish).

args.name: df
args.doc:
A `data.frame` or `tibble` containing SUS health data with ICD-10 codes.
  Typically obtained from DATASUS systems (SIM, SIH, SINAN). The data should
  contain at least one column with ICD-10 codes in standard format (e.g., "A00.0",
  "I10", "C50.9").

args.name: icd_codes
args.doc:
A character vector of ICD-10 codes, ranges, or categories.
  Multiple syntaxes are supported:

args.name: disease_group
args.doc:
Character. Name of predefined disease group (e.g., "dengue",
  "cardiovascular", "respiratory"). Use `list_disease_groups()` to see all available groups.
  Mutually exclusive with `icd_codes`.

args.name: icd_column
args.doc:
Character. Name of the column containing ICD-10 codes.
  If NULL (default), the function attempts auto-detection from common SUS
  column names in this priority order:
  1. `"CAUSABAS"` - Underlying cause (primary cause of death in SIM)
  2. `"DIAG_PRINC"` - Main diagnosis (SIH hospitalizations)
  3. `"DIAG_SECUN"` - Secondary diagnosis
  4. `"CAUSAOBITO"` - Cause of death (alternative SIM field)

args.name: match_type
args.doc:
Character. Type of matching algorithm:
  - `"exact"`: Exact code match (e.g., "I10" matches only "I10")
  - `"starts_with"`: Match codes starting with pattern (default, e.g., "I10" matches "I10", "I10.0", "I10.9")
  - `"range"`: Match codes within specified ranges (e.g., "I10-I15" matches I10-I15.9)
  - `"chapter"`: Match entire ICD-10 chapter (e.g., "I" matches I00-I99.9)
  - `"fuzzy"`: Allow for

args.name: backend
args.doc:
Character string specifying the data processing backend.
  Use `"arrow"` for out-of-memory, lazy processing (recommended for large datasets),
  or `"tibble"` for in-memory processing (recommended for small to medium datasets).

args.name: lang
args.doc:
Character. Language for user interface messages, warnings, and
  documentation. Options:
  - `"en"`: English
  - `"pt"`: Portuguese (default, recommended for Brazilian users)
  - `"es"`: Spanish
  Affects all console output and documentation of matched codes.

args.name: verbose
args.doc:
Logical. If TRUE (default), prints detailed filtering
  information including: records processed, match statistics, common
  coding issues detected, and time elapsed.

name: sus_data_filter_demographics
title: Filter Health Data by Demographic Variables
description:
Filters health data based on demographic characteristics such as sex, race,
age range, education level, region, and municipality. This function complements
`sus_data_filter_cid()` by enabling stratified analyses by population subgroups.

args.name: df
args.doc:
A `climasus_df` object containing health data (output of the
  `climasus4r` pipeline).

args.name: sex
args.doc:
Character vector specifying sex categories to include. Accepts
  values in English, Portuguese, or Spanish (e.g., `"Male"`, `"Masculino"`,
  `"Masculino"`). If `NULL` (default), includes all sexes.

args.name: race
args.doc:
Character vector specifying race/color categories to include.
  Accepts IBGE standard categories in multiple languages. If `NULL` (default),
  includes all races.

args.name: age_range
args.doc:
Numeric vector of length 2 specifying the age range
  `c(min_age, max_age)`. Use `Inf` for no upper limit. If `NULL` (default),
  includes all ages.

args.name: education
args.doc:
Character vector specifying education levels to include.
  If `NULL` (default), includes all education levels.

args.name: region
args.doc:
A string indicating a predefined group of states or regions
  (supports multilingual names PT, EN, ES). See Details.

args.name: city
args.doc:
Character vector of municipality names (e.g., `"Sao Paulo"`,
  `"Natal"`) or IBGE codes (6 or 7-digit, e.g., `"3550308"`, `"2408102"`).
  Case-insensitive; accents are normalised for matching. Partial typos trigger
  fuzzy suggestions. If `NULL` (default), no additional municipality filter is
  applied. Results are merged (union) with any codes in `municipality_code`.

args.name: municipality_code
args.doc:
Character or numeric vector specifying municipality
  codes (IBGE 6 or 7-digit codes) to include. If `NULL` (default), includes
  all municipalities.

args.name: drop_ignored
args.doc:
Logical. If `TRUE`, explicitly removes rows where
  demographic variables (sex, race, education) contain missing values (`NA`)
  or DATASUS ignored codes (e.g., `"9"`, `"Ignorado"`). Default is `FALSE`.

args.name: backend
args.doc:
Character string specifying the data processing backend.
  Use `"arrow"` for out-of-memory, lazy processing (recommended for large datasets),
  or `"tibble"` for in-memory processing (recommended for small to medium datasets).

args.name: use_cache
args.doc:
Logical. If `TRUE` (default), uses cached spatial data to
  avoid re-downloads and improve performance. Only relevant when `city` is
  provided.

args.name: cache_dir
args.doc:
Character string specifying the directory to store cached
  files. Default is `"~/.climasus4r_cache/spatial"`.

args.name: lang
args.doc:
Character string specifying the language for messages.
  Options: `"en"` (English), `"pt"` (Portuguese, default), `"es"` (Spanish).

args.name: verbose
args.doc: Logical. If `TRUE` (default), prints filtering summary.

name: sus_data_import
title: Import and preprocess data from DATASUS with intelligent caching
description:
This function acts as a wrapper for `microdatasus::fetch_datasus`,
simplifying the download and reading of data from Brazilian public health
information systems (SIM, SINAN, SIH, SIA, CNES, SINASC).
It includes parallel processing, caching, and user-friendly CLI feedback.

args.name: uf
args.doc:
A string or vector of strings with state abbreviations (**igonered if 'region' is provided**) 
  (e.g., "AM", c("SP", "RJ")). Valid UF codes: AC, AL, AP, AM, BA, CE, DF, ES, 
  GO, MA, MT, MS, MG, PA, PB, PR, PE, PI, RJ, RN, RS, RO, RR, SC, SP, SE, TO.

args.name: region
args.doc: A string indicating a predefined group of states (supports multilingual names PT, EN, ES). Available regions:

args.name: year
args.doc: An integer or vector of integers with the desired years (4 digits).

args.name: month
args.doc:
An integer or vector of integers with the desired months (1-12). 
  This argument is only used with monthly-based health information systems: 
  SIH, CNES, and SIA. For annual systems (SIM, SINAN, SINASC), this parameter 
  is ignored.

args.name: system
args.doc: A string indicating the information system. Available systems:

args.name: backend
args.doc:
Character string specifying the data processing backend.
  Use `"arrow"` for out-of-memory, lazy processing (recommended for large datasets),
  or `"tibble"` for in-memory processing (recommended for small to medium datasets).

args.name: city
args.doc:
Character vector of municipality names (e.g., `"Porto Velho"`,
  `"São Paulo"`). Case-insensitive; accents are normalised. Typos trigger
  fuzzy suggestions. Union with `municipality_code`. Applied after download
  to the combined dataset.

args.name: municipality_code
args.doc:
Character or numeric vector of 6 or 7-digit IBGE
  municipality codes. Applied independently from `city`; both 6-digit
  (DATASUS) and 7-digit (IBGE) forms are always included.

args.name: use_cache
args.doc:
Logical. If TRUE (default), will use cached data to avoid
  re-downloads. Cache is based on UF, year, month, and system parameters.

args.name: cache_dir
args.doc:
Character. Directory to store cached files. 
  Default is "~/.climasus4r_cache/data".

args.name: force_redownload
args.doc:
Logical. If TRUE, ignores cache and re-downloads 
  everything. Useful when you suspect cached data is corrupted or outdated.

args.name: parallel
args.doc:
Logical. If TRUE (default), will use parallel processing 
  for multiple UF/year combinations. Significantly speeds up bulk downloads.

args.name: workers
args.doc:
Integer. Number of parallel workers to use. Default is 4. 
  Set to 1 to disable parallel processing.

args.name: lang
args.doc:
Character string specifying the language for variable labels and
  messages. Options: `"en"` (English), `"pt"` (Portuguese, default), `"es"` (Spanish).

args.name: verbose
args.doc:
Logical. If TRUE (default), prints detailed progress 
  information including cache status, download progress, and time estimates.

name: sus_data_plot_aggregate_map
title: Plot Municipal Map of Aggregated Health Data

args.name: df
args.doc:
A `climasus_df` at `stage = "aggregate"` or later (output of
  [sus_data_aggregate()]).

args.name: value_col
args.doc:
Character.  Name of the health-count column to map.
  If `NULL` (default) the column is auto-detected using the same priority
  order as [sus_climate_plot_aggregate()]:
  `n_obitos`, `n_internacoes`, `n_nascimentos`, `n_casos`,
  `n_procedimentos`, `n_estabelecimentos` and their English / Spanish
  equivalents.

args.name: map_type
args.doc: Character.  `"bubble"` (default), `"choropleth"` or `"quantile_choropleth"`

args.name: rate_per_100k
args.doc:
Logical.  If `TRUE`, divides `total_cases` by `pop_25`
  (from `municipio_meta`) and multiplies by 1e5 to compute incidence per
  100,000 inhabitants.  Default `FALSE`.

args.name: period
args.doc:
Date scalar or `c(start, end)` vector.  When supplied, rows
  in `df` whose `date` column falls outside the interval are removed before
  aggregation.  `NULL` (default) uses all rows.

args.name: city
args.doc:
Character vector of municipality names or 6-/7-digit IBGE codes.
  When supplied, the map is restricted to those municipalities. Names are
  resolved through `municipio_meta` with accent tolerance.

args.name: top_n
args.doc:
Integer.  If supplied, only the top N municipalities by
  `total_cases` are labelled on the map.  `NULL` disables top-N labels.

args.name: state_borders
args.doc:
Logical.  Overlay state boundary polygons
  (downloaded via `geobr`).  Default `TRUE`.

args.name: show_labels
args.doc:
Logical.  Annotate capital cities with text labels
  (bubble map only).  Default `TRUE`.

args.name: palette
args.doc:
Character.  Colour palette for the fill / bubble fill scale.
  Any [RColorBrewer::brewer.pal()] sequential/diverging palette name
  (e.g. `"YlOrRd"`, `"Blues"`, `"RdYlBu"`) or one of the `ggsci` aliases
  `"lancet"` / `"nejm"`.  Default `"YlOrRd"`.

args.name: log_scale
args.doc:
Logical.  Apply `log1p` transformation to the colour
  scale.  Default `TRUE`.

args.name: title
args.doc:
Character.  Map title.  `NULL` uses a built-in multilingual
  default.

args.name: subtitle
args.doc:
Character.  Map subtitle.  `NULL` auto-generates one from
  the number of municipalities, metric and period.

args.name: caption
args.doc:
Character.  Figure caption.  `NULL` uses the DATASUS source
  string for the selected language.

args.name: theme_style
args.doc:
Character.  Reserved for future theme variants.
  Currently only `"publication"` (default) is implemented.

args.name: base_size
args.doc: Numeric.  Base font size for `theme_void()`.  Default `11`.

args.name: interactive
args.doc:
Logical.  If `TRUE`, wraps the ggplot2 object with
  `plotly::ggplotly()`.  Requires `plotly`.  Default `FALSE`.

args.name: use_cache
args.doc:
Logical.  Cache municipality metadata to disk.
  Default `TRUE`.

args.name: cache_dir
args.doc:
Character.  Directory for the disk cache.
  Default `"~/.climasus4r_cache/spatial"`.

args.name: lang
args.doc:
Character.  Language for messages and axis labels:
  `"pt"` (default), `"en"`, `"es"`.

args.name: verbose
args.doc: Logical.  Print progress messages.  Default `TRUE`.

name: sus_data_plot_aggregate_ts
title: Time-Series Plot of Aggregated DATASUS Health Outcomes
description:
Produces publication-quality time-series visualisations (epidemic curve,
seasonal boxplot, calendar heatmap, or annual trend) from a `climasus_df`
dataset at stage `"aggregate"` or later. Colour palettes are powered by
[ggsci] (Lancet, NEJM, JCO, UChicago). An interactive [plotly] widget can
be returned with `interactive = TRUE`.

args.name: df
args.doc:
A `climasus_df` at stage `"aggregate"` or later (output of
  [sus_data_aggregate()]).

args.name: value_col
args.doc:
Character. Name of the outcome column to plot. If `NULL`
  (default), the function auto-detects the first matching column from:
  `n_obitos`, `n_internacoes`, `n_nascimentos`, `n_casos`,
  `n_procedimentos`, `n_estabelecimentos`, and their English / Spanish
  equivalents.

args.name: group_col
args.doc:
Character. Optional column used for colour-grouping in
  `"epidemic"` and `"seasonal"` plots.

args.name: facet_col
args.doc: Character. Optional column for `facet_wrap()` panels.

args.name: facet_ncol
args.doc: Integer. Number of facet columns. Default `3L`.

args.name: plot_type
args.doc:
Character. One or more of `"epidemic"`, `"seasonal"`,
  `"heatmap"`, `"trend"`. When multiple types are supplied, plots are
  assembled with [patchwork] (one per row). Default: `"epidemic"`.

args.name: smooth_method
args.doc:
Character. Smoothing method for the `"epidemic"` plot:
  `"loess"` (default), `"gam"`, or `"lm"`.

args.name: smooth_span
args.doc: Numeric. Span for LOESS smoothing. Default `0.25`.

args.name: log_transform
args.doc:
Logical. Apply `log1p` transformation on the y-axis
  for `"epidemic"` and `"seasonal"` plots, and to the fill scale in
  `"heatmap"`. Default `FALSE`.

args.name: free_scales
args.doc:
Logical. Allow free y-axis scales in faceted plots.
  Default `TRUE`.

args.name: palette
args.doc:
Character. Colour palette. One of `"lancet"` (default),
  `"nejm"`, `"jco"`, `"uchicago"`, or `"viridis"`.

args.name: title
args.doc: Character. Plot title. Auto-generated if `NULL`.

args.name: subtitle
args.doc:
Character. Plot subtitle. `NULL` auto-generates one from
  row count, outcome column name and date range.

args.name: caption
args.doc:
Character. Figure caption. `NULL` uses the DATASUS source
  string for the selected language.

args.name: theme_style
args.doc:
Character. Reserved for future theme variants.
  Currently only `"publication"` (default) is implemented.

args.name: date_labels
args.doc:
Character. `strftime` format string for x-axis date
  labels. Default `"%b/%y"`.

args.name: year_breaks
args.doc:
Character. `date_breaks` string for the x-axis. Default
  `"3 months"`.

args.name: base_size
args.doc: Numeric. Base font size for the ggplot2 theme. Default `11`.

args.name: interactive
args.doc:
Logical. Return a [plotly::ggplotly()] widget instead of
  a static ggplot2 object. Default `FALSE`.

args.name: city
args.doc:
Character vector of municipality names or 6-/7-digit IBGE codes.
  When supplied, the plot is filtered to those municipalities before
  rendering. Names are resolved through `municipio_meta`, with accent
  tolerance, using the same cache as [sus_data_filter_demographics()].

args.name: use_cache
args.doc:
Logical. Cache municipality metadata to disk when city
  filtering or municipality labels are needed. Default `TRUE`.

args.name: cache_dir
args.doc:
Character. Directory for municipality metadata cache.
  Default `"~/.climasus4r_cache/spatial"`.

args.name: lang
args.doc:
Character. Language for labels and messages: `"pt"` (default),
  `"en"`, or `"es"`.

args.name: verbose
args.doc: Logical. Print progress messages. Default `TRUE`.

name: sus_data_plot_demographics
title: Visualise Demographic Profiles from DATASUS Systems
description:
Produces publication-quality tables, charts, and composite dashboards
summarising the demographic and climate-risk composition of any
standardised `climasus_df` dataset (SIM, SIH, SINAN, CNES, SIA, SINASC).
Visual style follows The Lancet / Nature Medicine guidelines.
Colour palettes are powered by [ggsci].

args.name: df
args.doc:
A `climasus_df` object at stage `"filter_demo"` or later (output
  of [sus_data_filter_demographics()]).

args.name: type
args.doc:
Character. Visualisation type. One of:
  - `"table"`      -- Frequency table (gt / DT).
  - `"bar"`        -- Horizontal bar chart for one variable.
  - `"pyramid"`    -- Age-sex population pyramid.
  - `"heatmap"`    -- Cross-demographic tile matrix (two variables).
  - `"temporal"`   -- Epidemic curve / time-series.
  - `"climate"`    -- Climate-risk group distribution (bar + season heatmap).

args.name: var
args.doc:
Character. Demographic variable (required for `"bar"` and
  single-variable `"table"`). One of `"sex"`, `"race"`, `"age_group"`,
  `"education"`, `"climate_risk"`, `"region"`.

args.name: time_unit
args.doc:
Character. Temporal resolution for `type = "temporal"`.
  One of `"month"` (default), `"epi_week"`, `"year"`, `"quarter"`,
  `"semester"`.

args.name: fill_var
args.doc:
Character. Optional stratification variable for temporal
  plots (e.g., `"sex"`, `"age_group"`, `"climate_risk_group"`).

args.name: palette
args.doc:
Character. Colour palette powered by [ggsci]. One of
  `"lancet"` (default), `"nature"`, `"nejm"`, `"jco"`, `"aaas"`,
  `"sus"`, `"viridis"`, `"colorblind"`.

args.name: heatmap_row
args.doc:
Character. Row variable for `type = "heatmap"`. One of
  `"age_group"`, `"sex"`, `"race"`, `"education"`, `"climate_risk"`,
  `"region"`. Defaults to `"age_group"` (auto-detected).

args.name: heatmap_col
args.doc:
Character. Column variable for `type = "heatmap"`.
  Defaults to `"race"` (auto-detected). Must differ from `heatmap_row`.

args.name: fill_metric
args.doc:
Character. What to show in each tile for
  `type = "heatmap"`. One of `"pct_row"` (default -- % within each row,
  e.g. % of each age group that is Parda), `"pct_col"` (% within each
  column), `"pct_total"` (% of all records), `"count"` (raw count).

args.name: show_ci
args.doc:
Logical. Add 95% Poisson confidence intervals in temporal
  plots. Default `FALSE`.

args.name: benchmark
args.doc:
Numeric named vector. Reference proportions for the
  race-equity plot. If `NULL` (default), IBGE 2022 Census proportions
  are used where available.

args.name: interactive
args.doc:
Logical. Return interactive [plotly::ggplotly()] widget
  instead of static ggplot2/gt objects. Default `FALSE`.

args.name: base_size
args.doc: Numeric. Base font size for ggplot2 theme. Default `11`.

args.name: lang
args.doc:
Character. Language for labels and messages. One of `"pt"`
  (default), `"en"`, `"es"`.

args.name: subtitle
args.doc: Character. Figure subtitle. `NULL` uses no subtitle.

args.name: caption
args.doc:
Character. Figure caption. `NULL` auto-generates a DATASUS
  source string for the selected language.

args.name: theme_style
args.doc:
Character. Reserved for future theme variants.
  Currently only `"publication"` (default) is implemented.

args.name: caption_suffix
args.doc:
Character. Additional text appended to the figure
  caption (e.g., study period, DOI). Default `NULL`.

args.name: save_path
args.doc:
Character. File path to save output (PNG, PDF, SVG, or
  HTML). Default `NULL` (no file saved).

args.name: width
args.doc:
Numeric. Output dimensions in inches. Defaults: `7 x 5`
  for single plots; `12 x 9` for dashboards.

args.name: height
args.doc:
Numeric. Output dimensions in inches. Defaults: `7 x 5`
  for single plots; `12 x 9` for dashboards.

args.name: dpi
args.doc: Numeric. Resolution for raster output. Default `300`.

args.name: verbose
args.doc: Logical. Print progress messages. Default `TRUE`.

name: sus_data_quality_report
title: Pipeline-aware data quality report for health data
description:
Reads `sus_meta` processing history to identify which climasus4r pipeline
functions were applied, then produces a section-by-section quality
assessment covering completeness, demographic distributions, date
validity, ICD-10 codes, geographic coverage, and derived variables.
Returns an overall quality score (0-100) and supports four output formats.

args.name: df
args.doc:
A `climasus_df` or plain `data.frame`. Arrow objects are
  materialised automatically.

args.name: output_format
args.doc:
Character. One of `"console"` (default), `"gt"`,
  `"markdown"`, or `"html"`. The `"gt"` format returns a `gt_tbl` object
  suitable for Quarto / R Markdown documents.

args.name: output_file
args.doc:
Character. File path for `"markdown"` and `"html"`
  formats. Defaults to a timestamped filename in the working directory.

args.name: check_icd
args.doc: Logical. Include ICD-10 quality section. Default `TRUE`.

args.name: check_dates
args.doc: Logical. Include date validation section. Default `TRUE`.

args.name: top_n
args.doc: Integer. Rows shown in frequency tables. Default `10`.

args.name: lang
args.doc: Character. Output language: `"pt"` (default), `"en"`, `"es"`.

args.name: verbose
args.doc: Logical. Print progress messages. Default `TRUE`.

name: sus_data_read
title: Read Processed Health Data with Batch and Parallel Support
description:
Smartly reads one or multiple health data files exported by `sus_data_export()`.
Supports automatic format detection, batch processing, parallel execution, spatial data,
metadata loading, and data validation.

args.name: path
args.doc:
Character vector of file paths, or a single directory path.
  If a directory is provided, all matching files will be read.

args.name: format
args.doc:
Character string specifying the input format. Options: `"dbf"`, `"dbc"`,
  `"rds"`, `"parquet"`, `"geoparquet"`, `"shapefile"`, `"gpkg"`, `"geojson"`, `"csv"`.
  If `NULL` (default), automatically detects format from file extension.

args.name: parallel
args.doc:
Logical. If `TRUE`, uses parallel processing for multiple files.
  Requires `future` and `future.apply` packages. Default: `FALSE`.

args.name: workers
args.doc: Integer. Number of parallel workers when `parallel = TRUE`. Default: 4.

args.name: read_metadata
args.doc:
Logical. If `TRUE` (default), loads companion metadata files
  and attaches them as attributes.

args.name: lang
args.doc:
Character string specifying the language for messages. Options:
  `"en"` (English), `"pt"` (Portuguese, default), `"es"` (Spanish).

args.name: verbose
args.doc: Logical. If `TRUE` (default), prints progress and summary.

name: sus_data_standardize
title: Standardize SUS data column names and values
description:
This function standardizes column names and categorical values in SUS datasets,
ensuring consistency across different years and versions. It supports three languages:
English (en), Portuguese (pt), and Spanish (es).

The function builds upon the preprocessing done by `microdatasus`, adding an
additional layer of standardization specifically designed for climate-health
research workflows.

args.name: df
args.doc: A `data.frame` or `tibble` to be standardized (typically output from `sus_data_import()`).

args.name: lang
args.doc:
Character. Output language for column names and values. Options: "en" (English),
  "pt" (Portuguese, Default), "es" (Spanish).

args.name: translate_columns
args.doc: Logical. If TRUE, translates column names. Default is TRUE.

args.name: standardize_values
args.doc: Logical. If TRUE, standardizes categorical values. Default is TRUE.

args.name: keep_original
args.doc: Logical. If TRUE, keeps original columns alongside standardized ones. Default is FALSE.

args.name: backend
args.doc:
Character string specifying the data processing backend.
  Use `"arrow"` for out-of-memory, lazy processing (recommended for large datasets),
  or `"tibble"` for in-memory processing (recommended for small to medium datasets).

args.name: verbose
args.doc: Logical. If TRUE, prints a report of standardization actions. Default is TRUE.

name: sus_data_ts_quality
title: Time-Series Quality Control for Daily Municipal Health Counts
description:
Evaluates the quality of daily health event time series at the municipal
level, producing per-municipality flags for:

- **Completeness**: fraction of expected days with non-missing records.
- **Structural breaks**: abrupt level shifts detectable by the CUSUM-based
  Zeileis et al. (2002) test (requires the `strucchange` package).
- **Monthly outliers**: months with counts > Q3 + 1.5 x IQR (Tukey fence).
- **Temporal gaps**: runs of consecutive days with zero *or* missing counts
  longer than `max_gap`.

A composite inclusion score (0-100) and a binary `include` recommendation
are returned, ma

args.name: data
args.doc:
A `climasus_df` at stage `"aggregate"`, or any `data.frame`
  with a municipality identifier column, a date column, and a daily count
  column.

args.name: outcome_col
args.doc:
Character. Name of the daily count column.
  Default: `"n_obitos"`.

args.name: muni_col
args.doc:
Character. Name of the municipality identifier column.
  Default: `"code_muni"`.

args.name: date_col
args.doc: Character. Name of the date column. Default: `"date"`.

args.name: min_completeness
args.doc:
Numeric (0-1). Minimum completeness to recommend
  inclusion. Default: `0.90` (90% of expected days present).

args.name: max_gap
args.doc:
Integer. Maximum tolerated consecutive zero/missing days
  before flagging a temporal gap. Default: `7L`.

args.name: break_alpha
args.doc:
Numeric. Significance level for structural break test.
  Default: `0.05`. Ignored if `strucchange` is not installed.

args.name: max_outlier_months
args.doc:
Integer. Maximum number of outlier months allowed
  before exclusion. Default: `3L`.

args.name: lang
args.doc:
Character. Language for messages: `"pt"` (default), `"en"`,
  `"es"`.

args.name: verbose
args.doc: Logical. Print progress messages. Default `TRUE`.

name: sus_grid_chirps
title: Import CHIRPS Rainfall Data for Brazilian Municipalities

args.name: resolution
years
args.doc:
Integer vector. Years to download. Coverage: 1981 to present
  for daily/monthly; 1981–2024 for annual. `NULL` defaults to the last
  two complete years.

args.name: months
args.doc:
Integer vector (1–12). Months to include for daily and monthly
  resolutions. Ignored for annual. Default `1:12`.

args.name: municipalities
args.doc:
An `sf` POLYGON object (e.g., from
  [geobr::read_municipality()]). When provided, rasters are aggregated and a
  `climasus_df` is returned. If `NULL`, a named character vector of cached
  file paths is returned instead.

args.name: agg_fun
args.doc:
Character. Spatial aggregation function for
  `exactextractr::exact_extract()`. Default `"mean"` (area-weighted mean).
  For rainfall totals over a municipality use `"sum"`.

args.name: crop_brazil
args.doc:
Logical. Crop global rasters to Brazil's bounding box
  before aggregation. Reduces memory usage significantly. Default `TRUE`.

args.name: use_cache
args.doc:
Logical. Reuse previously downloaded raster files and
  aggregated Parquet caches. Default `TRUE`.

args.name: cache_dir
args.doc:
Character. Root cache directory.
  Default `"~/.climasus4r_cache/chirps"`.

args.name: lang
args.doc: Character. Message language: `"pt"` (default), `"en"`, `"es"`.

args.name: verbose
args.doc: Logical. Print progress messages. Default `TRUE`.

name: sus_grid_era5
title: Import ERA5-Land Daily Climate Data for Brazilian Municipalities

args.name: years
args.doc:
Numeric vector of years to import. Must be between 1950 and 2025.
  Example: `2010:2023`, `c(2019, 2021)`.

args.name: months
args.doc:
Integer vector of months to import (1–12). Default: `1:12`
  (all months).

args.name: vars
args.doc:
Character vector of variable aliases to download. Allowed values:
  \itemize{
    \item `"t2m"` — 2 m temperature mean (→ `tair_dry_bulb_c`, °C)
    \item `"t2m_max"` — 2 m temperature maximum (→ `tair_max_c`, °C)
    \item `"t2m_min"` — 2 m temperature minimum (→ `tair_min_c`, °C)
    \item `"td2m"` — 2 m dewpoint temperature mean (→ `dew_point_c`, °C)
    \item `"u10"` — 10 m u-component of wind

args.name: municipalities
args.doc:
An `sf` object with polygon geometries representing the
  areas to which raster data will be aggregated (typically Brazilian
  municipalities from [geobr::read_municipality()]). Must contain a column
  identifying each polygon — the function auto-detects `code_muni`, `CD_MUN`,
  or `CD_GEOCMU`. If `NULL`, returns a named list of downloaded NetCDF file
  paths instead of a `climasus_df`.

args.name: agg_fun
args.doc:
Character. Spatial aggregation function applied over raster
  pixels within each polygon. Any function supported by
  `exactextractr::exact_extract()`: `"mean"` (default, area-weighted),
  `"sum"`, `"median"`, `"min"`, `"max"`. For precipitation, `"mean"` gives
  the representative areal value.

args.name: use_cache
args.doc:
Logical. If `TRUE` (default), downloaded NetCDF files are
  stored in `cache_dir` and reused on subsequent calls.

args.name: cache_dir
args.doc:
Character. Directory for cached NetCDF files.
  Default: `"~/.climasus4r_cache/era5"`. Created automatically if needed.

args.name: parallel
args.doc:
Logical. If `TRUE`, downloads run in parallel using the
  active [future::plan()]. Do not set a plan inside this function; configure
  it beforehand. Default: `FALSE`.

args.name: workers
args.doc:
Integer. Number of parallel workers when `parallel = TRUE`.
  Default: `2`.

args.name: lang
args.doc:
Character. Language for messages: `"pt"` (default), `"en"`,
  or `"es"`.

args.name: verbose
args.doc: Logical. If `TRUE` (default), prints progress messages.

name: sus_grid_fires
title: Import Fire Hotspot Data for Brazilian Municipalities

args.name: years
args.doc:
Numeric vector of years. INPE: 1998–present.
  FIRMS MODIS: 2000–present. FIRMS VIIRS: 2012–present.

args.name: months
args.doc: Integer vector (1–12). Default `1:12`.

args.name: uf
args.doc:
Character vector of state codes (e.g., `c("MT", "PA")`).
  Case-insensitive. `NULL` = all Brazil. Used to filter INPE queries and
  as a shortcut to define `bbox` for FIRMS.

args.name: bbox
args.doc:
Numeric vector `c(xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax)` in WGS84 longitude/
  latitude. Used by FIRMS for spatial subsetting. If `NULL` and `uf` is
  provided, the bbox is computed from Brazilian state centroids. If both
  are `NULL`, Brazil's bounding box is used.

args.name: source
args.doc:
Character. Data source: `"inpe"` (default), `"firms_modis"`,
  or `"firms_viirs"`.

args.name: municipalities
args.doc:
An `sf` POLYGON object (e.g., from
  [geobr::read_municipality()]). When provided, fire points are spatially
  joined to polygons and aggregated to daily municipality-level counts.
  If `NULL`, the raw fire point data are returned as a `climasus_df`.

args.name: agg_fun
args.doc:
Character. Aggregation strategy when `municipalities` is
  provided. Currently `"count"` is supported (count of fire hotspots per
  municipality per day). Future versions may support `"frp_sum"`.

args.name: biome
args.doc:
Character vector. INPE biome filter. Allowed values:
  `"Amazonia"`, `"Cerrado"`, `"Mata Atlantica"`, `"Caatinga"`,
  `"Pampa"`, `"Pantanal"`. `NULL` = no filter.

args.name: use_cache
args.doc:
Logical. Reuse previously downloaded CSV/JSON files.
  Default `TRUE`.

args.name: cache_dir
args.doc:
Character. Root cache directory.
  Default `"~/.climasus4r_cache/fires"`.

args.name: firms_key
args.doc:
Character. NASA FIRMS MAP KEY.
  Defaults to `Sys.getenv("FIRMS_MAP_KEY")`. Required when
  `source %in% c("firms_modis", "firms_viirs")`.

args.name: lang
args.doc: Character. Message language: `"pt"` (default), `"en"`, `"es"`.

args.name: verbose
args.doc: Logical. Print progress. Default `TRUE`.

name: sus_grid_join
title: Join Gridded Environmental Data to Health Data

args.name: health_data
args.doc:
A `climasus_df` at stage `"spatial"`, `"aggregate"`, or
  `"census"`. Must contain `code_muni` (character) and `date` (Date) columns.

args.name: grid_data
args.doc:
A `climasus_df` at stage `"climate"` produced by any

args.name: by

Character vector. Join key columns. Default `c("code_muni", "date")`.

  monthly health data — the join will broadcast the annual value to all months.

Character. Overrides `sus_meta$type` in the result.

args.name: lang
args.doc: Character. Message language: `"pt"` (default), `"en"`, `"es"`.

args.name: verbose
args.doc: Logical. Print progress messages. Default `TRUE`.

name: sus_grid_koppen
title: Assign Kopppen-Geiger Climate Zones to Brazilian Municipalities
description:
Adds a `zona_koppen` column to a `climasus_df` (or plain `data.frame`) with
municipality-level Koppen-Geiger climate classification following
Alvares et al. (2013), who produced the first high-resolution (1 km) Koppen
map for Brazil.

Two assignment modes are available:

- **`"approx"`** (default): rule-based assignment from municipality centroid
  coordinates stored in the built-in `municipio_meta` table. Fast and
  dependency-free, with ~85% accuracy at the municipal level.
- **`"exact"`**: spatial join between municipality centroids and a user-
  supplied `sf` polygon layer (`koppen_sf`). P

args.name: data
args.doc:
A `climasus_df` or `data.frame` containing a `code_muni` column
  (6- or 7-digit IBGE municipality code), **or** an `sf` object of Brazilian
  municipalities.

args.name: mode
args.doc:
Character. Assignment mode: `"approx"` (default, no extra
  dependencies) or `"exact"` (requires `sf` and `koppen_sf`).

args.name: koppen_sf
args.doc:
An `sf` polygon object with a column named `koppen`
  containing the zone codes (`"Af"`, `"Am"`, etc.). Required when
  `mode = "exact"`. The Alvares et al. (2013) shapefile is available at
  <https://doi.org/10.1127/0941-2948/2013/0507>. Ignored for
  `mode = "approx"`.

args.name: as_factor
args.doc:
Logical. Return `zona_koppen` as an ordered factor with
  canonical level order (`Af < Am < As < Aw < BSh < Cf < Cw`)?
  Default `TRUE`. Set to `FALSE` to get a plain character column.

args.name: lang
args.doc:
Character. Language for messages: `"pt"` (default), `"en"`,
  `"es"`.

args.name: verbose
args.doc: Logical. Print progress messages. Default `TRUE`.

name: sus_grid_pdsi

name: sus_grid_pollution_cams
title: Import CAMS Pollution Data for Brazilian Municipalities

Character vector. Pollutants to include. Allowed values:
  `"pm25"`, `"pm10"`, `"co"`, `"o3"`, `"no2"`, `"so2"`, or `"all"`.

name: sus_grid_pollution_ghap
title: Import GHAP High-Resolution Pollution Data for Brazilian Municipalities

args.name: pollutants
args.doc:
Character vector. Pollutants to download.
  Accepted: `"pm25"`, `"o3"`, `"co"`. Default: `"pm25"`.
  Note: `"no2"` is not yet publicly available.

args.name: resolution
args.doc:
Character. Temporal aggregation of source files.
  One of `"daily"`, `"monthly"`, or `"annual"`. Default: `"monthly"`.
  \itemize{
    \item `"daily"` — available for PM2.5 only (2017–2022). Each monthly
      ZIP (~3 GB) is downloaded, extracted to a temp directory, each daily
      NetCDF is aggregated, and the result is cached as Parquet. Subsequent
      calls read from Parquet without re-extr

args.name: years
args.doc:
Integer vector. Years to download. Availability depends on
  pollutant and resolution:
  \itemize{
    \item PM2.5 daily, monthly & annual: 2017–2022
    \item O3 annual: 2000–2020
    \item CO annual: 2019–2022
