  `NULL` (default) returns all available years for the selected pollutant.

args.name: months
args.doc:
Integer vector (1–12). Months to include when
  `resolution = "monthly"`. Ignored for annual data. Default: `1:12`.

args.name: municipalities
args.doc:
An `sf` object with polygon geometries
  (e.g., from [geobr::read_municipality()]). If provided, raster data are
  aggregated to these polygons and a `climasus_df` is returned.
  If `NULL`, a named character vector of cached NetCDF file paths is
  returned instead.

args.name: agg_fun
args.doc:
Character. Spatial aggregation function applied by
  `exactextractr::exact_extract()`. Default: `"mean"` (area-weighted).
  Other options: `"sum"`, `"median"`, `"min"`, `"max"`.

args.name: crop_brazil
args.doc:
Logical. Crop global rasters to Brazil's bounding box
  before extraction, significantly reducing memory use. Default `TRUE`.

args.name: use_cache
args.doc:
Logical. If `TRUE` (default), reuse previously downloaded
  NetCDF files and previously computed municipality-level Parquet caches.

args.name: cache_dir
args.doc:
Character. Root directory for all cached files.
  Default `"~/.climasus4r_cache/ghap"`. Sub-directories are created per
  pollutant.

args.name: lang
args.doc: Character. Message language: `"pt"` (default), `"en"`, `"es"`.

args.name: verbose
args.doc: Logical. Print progress messages. Default `TRUE`.

name: sus_grid_pollution_merra2
title: Import MERRA-2 Pollution Data for Brazilian Municipalities

args.name: pollutants
args.doc:
Character vector. Variables to download.
  Allowed: `"pm25"`, `"aod"`, `"so2"`. Default: `c("pm25", "aod")`.

args.name: resolution
args.doc:
Character. Temporal resolution of source files.
  One of:
  \itemize{
    \item `"monthly"` (default) — uses `M2TMNXAER`, one file per month,
      already monthly-averaged. Recommended for most analyses.
    \item `"daily"` — uses `M2T1NXAER` (hourly), one file per day; 24
      hourly layers are aggregated to a single daily value using `agg_fun`.
      Note: `so2` only available in monthly resol

args.name: years
args.doc:
Integer vector. Years to download (1980 to current year).
  `NULL` defaults to the last two complete years.

args.name: months
args.doc: Integer vector (1–12). Months to include. Default `1:12`.

args.name: municipalities
args.doc:
An `sf` POLYGON object (e.g., from
  [geobr::read_municipality()]). If provided, raster data are aggregated
  and a `climasus_df` is returned. If `NULL`, returns cached NetCDF paths.

args.name: agg_fun
args.doc:
Character. Spatial aggregation function for
  `exactextractr::exact_extract()`. Default `"mean"` (area-weighted).

args.name: earthdata_user
args.doc:
Character. Earthdata username. Defaults to
  `Sys.getenv("EARTHDATA_USER")`.

args.name: earthdata_pass
args.doc:
Character. Earthdata password. Defaults to
  `Sys.getenv("EARTHDATA_PASSWORD")`.

args.name: netrc_path
args.doc:
Character. Path to a `.netrc` file with Earthdata
  credentials. If provided, takes precedence over `earthdata_user` /
  `earthdata_pass`.

args.name: crop_brazil
args.doc:
Logical. Crop rasters to Brazil's bounding box before
  extraction to save memory. Default `TRUE`.

args.name: use_cache
args.doc:
Logical. Reuse previously downloaded NetCDF files and
  previously computed Parquet caches. Default `TRUE`.

args.name: cache_dir
args.doc:
Character. Root cache directory.
  Default `"~/.climasus4r_cache/merra2"`.

args.name: lang
args.doc: Character. Message language: `"pt"` (default), `"en"`, `"es"`.

args.name: verbose
args.doc: Logical. Print progress messages. Default `TRUE`.

name: sus_grid_prodes
title: Import PRODES Deforestation Data from TerraBrasilis for Brazilian Municipalities

args.name: years
args.doc:
Integer vector. Years to download. Availability by biome:
  Amazon 2007–present; all others 2000–present.

args.name: biomes
args.doc:
Character vector. Biomes to include. Any combination of:
  `"Amazon"`, `"Cerrado"`, `"MataAtlantica"`, `"Caatinga"`, `"Pampa"`,
  `"Pantanal"`. Default: all six biomes.

args.name: uf
args.doc:
Character vector. Optional state filter (e.g., `c("MT", "PA")`).
  Reduces download size. `NULL` = all states in the selected biome(s).

args.name: municipalities
args.doc:
An `sf` POLYGON object (e.g., from
  [geobr::read_municipality()]). When provided, deforestation polygons are
  spatially intersected with municipality boundaries and annual areas are
  aggregated. If `NULL`, raw deforestation polygons are returned as an sf
  `climasus_df`.

args.name: use_cache
args.doc:
Logical. Reuse previously downloaded GeoJSON files and
  aggregated Parquet caches. Default `TRUE`.

args.name: cache_dir
args.doc:
Character. Root cache directory.
  Default `"~/.climasus4r_cache/prodes"`.

args.name: lang
args.doc: Character. Message language: `"pt"` (default), `"en"`, `"es"`.

args.name: verbose
args.doc: Logical. Print progress. Default `TRUE`.

name: sus_grid_smvi
title: Import Soil Moisture Volatility Index (SMVI / Flash Drought) Data

args.name: years
args.doc:
Integer vector. Years to include. Must be within 1990–2021.
  `NULL` (default) returns all available years.

args.name: municipalities
args.doc:
An `sf` POLYGON object (e.g., from
  [geobr::read_municipality()]). When provided, flash drought events are
  spatially assigned to municipalities and statistics are aggregated.
  If `NULL`, returns the raw event data frame for Brazil filtered to
  the requested years.

args.name: aggregate_by
args.doc:
Character. Temporal resolution of the output when
  `municipalities` is provided. One of `"year"` (default) or `"month"`.
  Annual aggregation counts flash drought events and their total duration
  per municipality per year.

args.name: brazil_only
args.doc:
Logical. Filter the global dataset to Brazil's bounding
  box (-75 to -28 lon, -35 to 6 lat) before processing. Default `TRUE`.

args.name: use_cache
args.doc: Logical. Reuse cached files. Default `TRUE`.

args.name: cache_dir
args.doc:
Character. Root cache directory.
  Default `"~/.climasus4r_cache/smvi"`.

args.name: lang
args.doc: Character. Message language: `"pt"` (default), `"en"`, `"es"`.

args.name: verbose
args.doc: Logical. Print progress messages. Default `TRUE`.

name: sus_mod_af
title: Attributable Fraction and Number from a DLNM Fit
description:
Computes population attributable fractions (AF) and attributable numbers (AN)
from a `climasus_dlnm` object returned by [sus_mod_dlnm()]. Decomposes the
total burden into heat and cold components, reports AF at user-defined
exposure percentile ranges, and optionally breaks the burden down by month,
year, or season.

args.name: fit
args.doc: A `climasus_dlnm` object produced by [sus_mod_dlnm()].

args.name: threshold
args.doc:
Numeric or `NULL`. Exposure value splitting heat (above) and
  cold (below) components. `NULL` (default) uses `fit$meta$ref_value`.

args.name: range
args.doc:
Numeric vector `c(low, high)` or `NULL`. When provided, AF is
  also computed for this custom exposure range.

args.name: pred_at
args.doc:
Numeric vector (0-1). Quantiles defining the percentile-band
  table. For each `q`, the AF above the `q`-th quantile (hot) and below the
  `(1-q)`-th quantile (cold) is reported. Default `c(0.75, 0.90, 0.95, 0.99)`.

args.name: by
args.doc:
Character or `NULL`. Temporal grouping for the period table:
  `"month"`, `"year"`, or `"season"`. `NULL` (default) skips the breakdown.

args.name: nsim
args.doc:
Integer. Number of Monte Carlo simulations for CI. Default
  `1000L`. Reduce to `200L` for quick exploratory runs; increase to `5000L`
  for publication-quality CI.

args.name: alpha
args.doc: Numeric. Significance level for CI. Default `0.05` (95%).

args.name: lang
args.doc: Character. Language for messages: `"pt"` (default), `"en"`, `"es"`.

args.name: verbose
args.doc: Logical. Print progress messages. Default `TRUE`.

name: sus_mod_burden
title: Ranked Disease Burden Table Across Cities or Strata
description:
Aggregates city-level attributable fraction or excess mortality/morbidity
results into a ranked burden table showing each city's contribution to the
total population burden. Accepts `climasus_af`, `climasus_excess`, or
`climasus_dlnm` objects (DLNM fits are automatically converted to AF via
[sus_mod_af()]). Produces a concentration curve suitable for Lorenz-style
inequality analysis: how unevenly burden is distributed across cities.

args.name: fits
args.doc:
Named list of `climasus_af`, `climasus_excess`, or
  `climasus_dlnm` objects. Names become city/stratum labels in the output.
  All elements must be the same class; mixing types is not allowed.

args.name: component
args.doc:
Character. For `climasus_af` inputs: which heat/cold
  component to display and rank. One of `"total"` (default), `"heat"`,
  `"cold"`, or `"all"` (all three components; ranking is always derived
  from the `"total"` component). Ignored for `climasus_excess` inputs.

args.name: rank_by
args.doc:
Character or `NULL`. Metric for ranking cities.
  For AF inputs: `"an"` (attributable number, default) or `"af_pct"`
  (attributable fraction percent). For excess inputs: `"excess"` (default)
  or `"excess_pct"`. `NULL` selects the default for the detected input type.

args.name: top_n
args.doc:
Integer or `NULL`. Keep only the top-N cities after ranking.
  `NULL` (default) retains all cities.

args.name: nsim
args.doc:
Integer. Monte Carlo simulations used when auto-computing AF
  from `climasus_dlnm` inputs. `0L` (default) uses the faster delta-method CI.

args.name: alpha
args.doc:
Numeric. Significance level for confidence intervals.
  Default `0.05`.

args.name: lang
args.doc:
Character. Language for messages: `"pt"` (default), `"en"`,
  `"es"`.

args.name: verbose
args.doc: Logical. Print progress messages. Default `TRUE`.

name: sus_mod_casecrossover
title: Time-Stratified Case-Crossover Analysis for Climate-Health Data
description:
Fits a time-stratified case-crossover model to quantify the association
between a climate exposure and a daily health outcome. Each time stratum
(month or week) acts as its own control, removing long-term trends and
seasonal confounding by design. Two fitting methods are supported:
`"conditional_poisson"` (GLM with stratum fixed effects, default) for
aggregate count data, and `"clogit"` (conditional logistic regression) for
binary outcomes or rare events.

args.name: data
args.doc:
A `climasus_df` at stage `"aggregate"` or `"climate"` (produced
  by `sus_climate_aggregate()`), or any `data.frame` containing at minimum a
  `date` column (Date), an outcome count column, and an exposure column.

args.name: outcome_col
args.doc:
Character. Name of the daily health outcome count column
  (e.g., `"n_obitos"`, `"n_internacoes"`). Default: `"n_obitos"`.

args.name: exposure_col
args.doc:
Character. Name of the exposure column. Required; no
  auto-detection is performed (unlike `sus_mod_dlnm()`). Examples:
  `"tair_dry_bulb_c"`, `"pm25_mean"`, `"precip_mm"`.

args.name: covariates
args.doc:
Character vector or `NULL`. Names of additional columns to
  include as linear confounders. These should be time-varying within strata
  (e.g., day-of-week dummy, holiday indicator, relative humidity). Long-term
  trends are already controlled by the strata. Default: `NULL`.

args.name: stratum
args.doc:
Character. Time stratum type or name of an existing column:
  - `"month"` (default): year-month strata (e.g., `"2021-07"`).
  - `"week"`: ISO year-week strata (e.g., `"2021-W27"`).
  - Any column name in `data`: uses that column's values as stratum IDs.

args.name: lag
args.doc:
Integer or integer vector. Lag(s) to apply to the exposure before
  fitting. A single integer uses that specific lag; a vector uses the mean
  of exposures at all specified lags. Default: `0L` (same-day exposure).

args.name: method
args.doc:
Character. Fitting method:
  - `"conditional_poisson"` (default): GLM with stratum fixed effects.
    Preferred for aggregate count data; uses `stats::glm()` with the
    specified `family`.
  - `"clogit"`: conditional logistic regression via `survival::clogit()`.
    Requires the `survival` package. Treats outcome as binary
    (`is_case = count > 0`); best for rare events.

args.name: family
args.doc:
Character. GLM family for `method = "conditional_poisson"`:
  `"quasipoisson"` (default, robust to overdispersion) or `"poisson"`.
  Ignored for `method = "clogit"`.

args.name: alpha
args.doc:
Numeric. Significance level for confidence intervals.
  Default: `0.05` (95% CI).

args.name: lang
args.doc:
Character. Language for messages: `"pt"` (default), `"en"`,
  `"es"`.

args.name: verbose
args.doc: Logical. Print progress messages. Default `TRUE`.

name: sus_mod_dlnm
title: Distributed Lag Non-linear Model (DLNM) for Climate-Health Analyses
description:
Fits a Distributed Lag Non-linear Model (DLNM) to quantify the association
between a climate exposure and a daily health outcome count. The function
accepts a `climasus_df` at stage `"climate"` / type `"distributed_lag"`
produced by `sus_climate_aggregate(temporal_strategy = "distributed_lag")`,
constructs the bidimensional `crossbasis`, fits a GLM, and returns a
`climasus_dlnm` object with the fitted model, pre-computed `crosspred`,
exposure-response and lag-response tables, and diagnostic statistics.

args.name: df
args.doc:
A `climasus_df` at stage `"climate"` and type `"distributed_lag"`,
  produced by `sus_climate_aggregate(temporal_strategy = "distributed_lag")`.
  Must contain a `date` column (Date), the outcome column, and lag columns
  `{climate_col}_lag0`, ..., `{climate_col}_lag{L}`. Geometry columns
  (sf) are automatically dropped before modelling.

args.name: outcome_col
args.doc:
Character. Name of the daily health count column
  (e.g., `"n_obitos"`, `"n_internacoes"`, `"n_dengue"`). Default:
  `"n_obitos"`.

args.name: climate_col
args.doc:
Character or `NULL`. Base name of the climate exposure
  (without `_lagN` suffix, e.g., `"tair_dry_bulb_c"`). `NULL` auto-detects
  by priority: air temperature > rainfall > humidity > other known variables.

args.name: lag_max
args.doc:
Integer or `NULL`. Maximum lag to include in the crossbasis.
  `NULL` auto-detects from the highest `N` present in `{climate_col}_lagN`
  columns.

args.name: covariates
args.doc:
Character vector or `NULL`. Names of additional columns in
  `df` to include as linear confounders in the GLM formula (e.g.,
  `c("rainfall_mm", "rh_mean_porc")`). These are averaged across
  municipalities before fitting. Default: `NULL`.

args.name: argvar
args.doc:
Named list. Arguments for the exposure dimension of the
  `crossbasis` (passed to `dlnm::crossbasis(argvar = ...)`). Default:
  `list(fun = "ns", df = 4)`. See **Basis selection** section.

args.name: arglag
args.doc:
Named list. Arguments for the lag dimension of the
  `crossbasis` (passed to `dlnm::crossbasis(arglag = ...)`). Default:
  `list(fun = "ns", df = 3)`. See **Basis selection** section.

args.name: family
args.doc:
Character. GLM family: `"quasipoisson"` (default, recommended),
  `"poisson"`, or `"negbin"` (redirected to `"quasipoisson"` with warning).

args.name: ns_df
args.doc:
Integer or `NULL`. Degrees of freedom for the natural spline
  time trend (`splines::ns(date, df = ns_df)`). `NULL` (default) triggers
  automatic computation based on `dof_per_year`. Set to `0` to suppress
  time control entirely.

args.name: dof_per_year
args.doc:
Integer. Degrees of freedom per year of data used for
  automatic `ns_df` computation. Default: `4L`. Ignored when `ns_df` is
  explicitly set.

args.name: ref_value
args.doc:
Numeric or `NULL`. Exposure value where RR = 1 (the
  centring value for `dlnm::crosspred()`). `NULL` (default) uses the
  sample median of lag-0 exposure values.

args.name: pred_at
args.doc:
Numeric vector of quantile probabilities (0-1) at which to
  report the cumulative exposure-response curve in `$exposure_response`.
  Default: `c(0.25, 0.50, 0.75, 0.90, 0.95, 0.99)`.

args.name: alpha
args.doc:
Numeric (0-1). Confidence level for intervals:
  CI = \eqn{(1 - \alpha)}. Default: `0.05` (95% CI).

args.name: lang
args.doc:
Character. Language for messages: `"pt"` (default), `"en"`,
  `"es"`.

args.name: verbose
args.doc: Logical. Print progress steps and warnings. Default `TRUE`.

name: sus_mod_excess
title: Excess Mortality and Morbidity from Climate-Health Time Series
description:
Estimates excess counts (observed minus expected baseline) for daily health
outcome series in the context of climate-health research. Supports three
baseline methods: counterfactual extraction from a fitted DLNM
(`"from_dlnm"`), quasi-Poisson GLM with natural splines (`"spline"`), and
Serfling sinusoidal regression (`"serfling"`). Confidence intervals are
derived from the baseline model's prediction standard errors. A z-score
flag identifies statistically significant excess events.

args.name: data
args.doc:
A `climasus_dlnm` object (for `method = "from_dlnm"`), a
  `climasus_df` at stage `"aggregate"` or later, or any `data.frame` with
  a date column and an integer count column.

args.name: outcome_col
args.doc:
Character. Name of the outcome count column. Required
  when `data` is a data frame; inferred from `fit$meta$outcome_col` when
  `data` is a `climasus_dlnm`.

args.name: date_col
args.doc: Character. Name of the date column. Default `"date"`.

args.name: control_period
args.doc:
Date vector of length 2 specifying the reference
  period used to fit the baseline model, e.g.
  `c(as.Date("2018-01-01"), as.Date("2019-12-31"))`. Default `NULL` uses
  all available data before `study_period`, or all data when both are `NULL`.

args.name: study_period
args.doc:
Date vector of length 2 specifying the period for which
  excess is reported. Default `NULL` uses the full data range.

args.name: method
args.doc:
Character. Baseline estimation method:
  - `"from_dlnm"` — counterfactual from DLNM (requires `climasus_dlnm`).
  - `"spline"` (default for data frames) — quasi-Poisson with ns().
  - `"serfling"` — Serfling harmonic regression.

args.name: dof_per_year
args.doc:
Integer. Degrees of freedom per year for the spline
  baseline. Default `8L`.

args.name: harmonics
args.doc:
Integer. Number of sinusoidal harmonics in the Serfling
  model. Default `2L`.

args.name: family
args.doc:
Character. GLM family: `"quasipoisson"` (default) or
  `"poisson"`. Ignored for `method = "from_dlnm"`.

args.name: threshold_z
args.doc:
Numeric. Z-score threshold for flagging excess events.
  Default `1.96` (95th percentile).

args.name: by
args.doc:
Character or `NULL`. Temporal breakdown variable for the period
  summary: `"year"`, `"month"`, or `"season"`. Default `NULL`.

args.name: alpha
args.doc:
Numeric. Significance level for confidence intervals.
  Default `0.05`.

args.name: lang
args.doc:
Character. Language for messages: `"pt"` (default), `"en"`,
  `"es"`.

args.name: verbose
args.doc: Logical. Print progress messages. Default `TRUE`.

name: sus_mod_its
title: Interrupted Time Series Analysis for Health Outcome Counts
description:
Fits a segmented quasi-Poisson regression to estimate the immediate and
sustained effects of one or more interruptions (policies, extreme events,
pandemics) on a daily health outcome count series. The model decomposes
each interruption into a **level change** (immediate step) and a **slope
change** (sustained trend shift), controlling for the underlying time trend
and seasonal variation via harmonic terms. A counterfactual projection shows
what would have been expected had the interruption not occurred.

args.name: data
args.doc:
A `climasus_df` at stage `"aggregate"` or `"climate"`, or any
  `data.frame` with a date column and an integer count column.

args.name: outcome_col
args.doc:
Character. Name of the daily health outcome count column.
  Default: `"n_obitos"`.

args.name: date_col
args.doc: Character. Name of the date column. Default: `"date"`.

args.name: interruption_dates
args.doc:
Date or character vector of at least one
  interruption date (ISO `"YYYY-MM-DD"` format). Must lie strictly within
  the data range. For a single interruption pass a scalar;
  for multiple, pass a vector.

args.name: harmonics
args.doc:
Integer. Number of sinusoidal harmonic pairs for seasonal
  control (\eqn{H} in the model above). `0` suppresses seasonal terms.
  Default: `2L`.

args.name: family
args.doc:
Character. GLM family: `"quasipoisson"` (default, robust to
  overdispersion) or `"poisson"`.

args.name: covariates
args.doc:
Character vector or `NULL`. Names of additional columns to
  include as linear confounders (e.g., `"rh_pct"`, `"holiday"`). Must be
  numeric or dummy-coded. Default: `NULL`.

args.name: alpha
args.doc:
Numeric. Significance level for confidence intervals.
  Default: `0.05` (95% CI).

args.name: counterfactual
args.doc:
Logical. Compute and return counterfactual predictions.
  Default: `TRUE`.

args.name: lang
args.doc:
Character. Language for messages: `"pt"` (default), `"en"`,
  `"es"`.

args.name: verbose
args.doc: Logical. Print progress messages. Default `TRUE`.

name: sus_mod_metaregression
title: Meta-Regression of Pooled DLNM Estimates with City-Level Covariates
description:
Extends two-stage multivariate meta-analysis (`sus_mod_pool()`) by including
city-level covariates (e.g., mean temperature, poverty index, age structure)
to explain between-city heterogeneity in climate-health associations.
Covariates are automatically standardized to mean 0, SD 1 so the intercept
represents the predicted association for an "average city". Returns
covariate Wald tests, residual heterogeneity statistics, and BLUP
predictions per city adjusted for covariate information.

args.name: fits
args.doc:
A named list of `climasus_dlnm` objects, one per city or region.
  All fits must use the same `climate_col`, `lag_max`, `argvar`, and
  `arglag`. Produced by `sus_mod_dlnm()`.

args.name: covariates
args.doc:
A `data.frame` with one row per city and city-level
  predictors. City names must be stored as row names (default) or in the
  column specified by `city_col`. Cities present in `fits` but absent in
  `covariates` are excluded from the meta-regression with a warning.

args.name: covariate_cols
args.doc:
Character vector of column names to use as
  meta-regression predictors. Default `NULL` uses all numeric columns in
  `covariates` (after removing the city identifier column if any).

args.name: city_col
args.doc:
Character. Name of the column in `covariates` that holds
  city identifiers. Default `NULL` uses row names of `covariates`.

args.name: pred_at
args.doc:
Numeric vector of quantile probabilities (0–1) for the
  exposure-response summary table. Default `c(0.75, 0.90, 0.95, 0.99)`.

args.name: blup
args.doc: Logical. Compute BLUP city-specific predictions? Default `TRUE`.

args.name: method
args.doc:
Character. `mvmeta` estimation method: `"reml"` (default),
  `"ml"`, or `"fixed"`.

args.name: alpha
args.doc:
Numeric. Significance level for confidence intervals.
  Default `0.05`.

args.name: lang
args.doc:
Character. Language for messages: `"pt"` (default), `"en"`,
  `"es"`.

args.name: verbose
args.doc: Logical. Print progress messages. Default `TRUE`.

name: sus_mod_ml
title: XGBoost Machine Learning for Climate-Health Outcome Prediction
description:
Trains an XGBoost gradient-boosted tree model to predict health outcomes
(disease counts, mortality, hospitalizations) from climate and socioeconomic
features. Accepts any aggregated data frame produced by the climasus4r
pipeline (or a plain `data.frame`). Uses k-fold cross-validation to select
the optimal number of trees (`nrounds`) and returns out-of-fold (OOF)
predictions alongside the final model trained on full data.

This function complements the DLNM epidemiological pipeline
([sus_mod_dlnm()]): DLNM models the exposure-response relationship and
provides causal inference; `sus_mod_ml()`

args.name: df
args.doc:
A data frame (plain or `climasus_df`) with one row per
  observation (e.g., city × day or city × month). Must contain
  `outcome_col` and at least one numeric feature column.

args.name: outcome_col
args.doc:
Character. Name of the column to predict (the target
  variable). Must be numeric.

args.name: feature_cols
args.doc:
Character vector or `NULL`. Names of columns to use as
  predictors. When `NULL` (default), all numeric columns except
  `outcome_col` and `id_col` are used automatically.

args.name: id_col
args.doc:
Character or `NULL`. City or group identifier column used for
  **group-aware cross-validation**: each group is kept entirely in one fold,
  preventing data leakage across cities. When `NULL`, standard random
  k-fold is used. Strongly recommended when data has a city dimension.

args.name: objective
args.doc:
Character. XGBoost objective function. One of
  `"count:poisson"` (default), `"reg:squarederror"`, or
  `"binary:logistic"`. See Details.

args.name: nrounds
args.doc:
Integer. Maximum number of boosting rounds. The optimal
  value is selected via early stopping during cross-validation. Default
  `500L`.

args.name: max_depth
args.doc:
Integer. Maximum tree depth. Default `6L`. Increase for
  more complex patterns; decrease to reduce overfitting.

args.name: eta
args.doc:
Numeric. Learning rate (step size shrinkage). Default `0.05`.
  Smaller values require more rounds but often generalize better.

args.name: subsample
args.doc:
Numeric ∈ (0, 1\]. Row subsampling ratio per tree.
  Default `0.8`.

args.name: colsample_bytree
args.doc:
Numeric ∈ (0, 1\]. Column subsampling ratio per
  tree. Default `0.8`.

args.name: min_child_weight
args.doc:
Numeric. Minimum sum of instance weight in a leaf.
  Default `1`. Increase to prevent learning rare patterns.

args.name: nfold
args.doc: Integer. Number of cross-validation folds. Default `5L`.

args.name: early_stopping
args.doc:
Integer. Stop training if the CV metric does not
  improve for this many consecutive rounds. Default `50L`.

args.name: seed
args.doc: Integer. Random seed for reproducibility. Default `42L`.

args.name: lang
args.doc:
Character. Language for messages: `"pt"` (default), `"en"`,
  `"es"`.

args.name: verbose
args.doc: Logical. Print progress messages. Default `TRUE`.

from a `climasus_dlnm` object returned by [sus_mod_dlnm()]. Implements all
visualisation types standard in distributed lag non-linear model (DLNM)
epidemiology: overall exposure-response, lag-response, bidimensional surface,

args.name: fit

args.name: type

name: sus_mod_plot_spacetime
title: Visualizations for Space-Time Bayesian Disease Mapping Results
description:
Produces five types of visualization for `climasus_spacetime_bayes` and
`climasus_spacetime_exceedance` objects returned by
[sus_mod_spacetime_bayes()] and [sus_mod_spacetime_exceedance()]:
choropleth maps of relative risk, temporal trend charts, space-time
interaction heatmaps, exceedance probability maps, and fixed-effect forest
plots. Static ggplot2 output is returned by default; set
`interactive = TRUE` for [plotly::ggplotly()] output.

args.name: x
args.doc:
A `climasus_spacetime_bayes` object from [sus_mod_spacetime_bayes()]
  or a `climasus_spacetime_exceedance` object from
  [sus_mod_spacetime_exceedance()].

args.name: type
args.doc:
Character. One of `"rr_map"` (default), `"temporal"`,
  `"interaction"`, `"exceedance"`, `"coef"`. See **Plot types**.

args.name: municipalities
args.doc:
An `sf` polygon object with a `code_muni` column
  (7-digit IBGE code). Required for `type = "rr_map"` and
  `type = "exceedance"`. Typically from [geobr::read_municipality()].

args.name: time_point
args.doc:
Integer or `NULL`. Single time index to display in
  `type = "rr_map"`. Ignored when `facet_time = TRUE` or for other types.
  Default `NULL` (aggregate mean across all periods).

args.name: time_range
args.doc:
Integer vector of length 2 (`c(t_start, t_end)`) to
  restrict the time axis in `type = "temporal"` and `type = "interaction"`.
  Default `NULL` (all periods).

args.name: threshold
args.doc:
Numeric. RR threshold used to select the exceedance layer
  from `x$exceedance` when `type = "exceedance"`. Default `1.0`.

args.name: facet_time
args.doc:
Logical. When `TRUE` and `type = "rr_map"`, produces a
  faceted map with one panel per time period. Default `FALSE`.

args.name: palette
args.doc:
Character. Name of a diverging RColorBrewer palette for RR
  maps (e.g. `"RdYlBu"`, `"RdBu"`, `"PuOr"`). Default `"RdYlBu"`.

args.name: title
args.doc:
Character or `NULL`. Custom plot title. `NULL` uses a
  multilingual default.

args.name: base_size
args.doc: Numeric. Base font size for ggplot2 themes. Default `11`.

args.name: interactive
args.doc:
Logical. `TRUE` wraps the ggplot object with
  [plotly::ggplotly()] (requires \pkg{plotly}). Default `FALSE`.

args.name: lang
args.doc:
Character. Language for axis labels and titles: `"pt"`
  (default), `"en"`, `"es"`.

description:

args.name: alpha

name: sus_mod_plot_spatial_scan
title: Choropleth Map of Kulldorff Spatial Scan Cluster Results
description:
Renders a choropleth map from a `climasus_spatial_scan` object produced by

args.doc:
A `climasus_spatial_scan` object returned by
  [sus_mod_spatial_scan()].

args.name: municipalities
args.doc:
An `sf` object with POLYGON or MULTIPOLYGON geometry
  containing a `code_muni` column that matches the identifiers stored in
  `x$most_likely_cluster$location_ids` and `x$secondary_clusters`.

args.name: show_rr
args.doc:
Logical.  `TRUE` (default) colours municipalities by
  Relative Risk using a continuous diverging scale.  `FALSE` uses
  categorical cluster-membership colours.

args.name: alpha
args.doc:
Numeric in (0, 1).  Significance threshold used to decide
  which cluster borders to emphasise.  Must match the value used when
  running [sus_mod_spatial_scan()].  Default `0.05`.

args.name: title
args.doc:
Character or `NULL`.  Plot title.  If `NULL` (default) a
  localised default title is used.

args.name: lang
args.doc: Character.  Output language: `"pt"` (default), `"en"`, `"es"`.

name: sus_mod_pool
title: Two-Stage Multi-City Pooling of DLNM Estimates
description:
Performs a two-stage multivariate meta-analysis to pool Distributed Lag
Non-linear Model (DLNM) estimates across multiple cities or regions.
Stage 1 city-specific estimates (coefficients and variance-covariance matrices
from `sus_mod_dlnm()`) are combined using multivariate random-effects
meta-analysis via `mvmeta`, producing pooled exposure-response and
lag-response curves, heterogeneity statistics, and Best Linear Unbiased
Predictors (BLUPs) for each city.

args.name: fits
args.doc:
A named list of `climasus_dlnm` objects, one per city or region.
  All fits must have been produced with the same `climate_col`, `argvar`,
  `arglag`, and `lag_max`.

args.name: exposure_range
args.doc:
Numeric vector of length 2 specifying the exposure
  grid range for the pooled prediction. Default `NULL` uses the combined
  range across all city datasets.

args.name: n_grid
args.doc:
Integer. Number of exposure grid points for the pooled
  prediction curve. Default `100L`.

args.name: pred_at
args.doc:
Numeric vector of quantile probabilities (0-1) for the
  pooled exposure-response summary table. Default `c(0.75, 0.90, 0.95, 0.99)`.

args.name: blup
args.doc: Logical. Compute BLUP city-specific predictions? Default `TRUE`.

args.name: method
args.doc:
Character. `mvmeta` estimation method: `"reml"` (default,
  recommended), `"ml"`, or `"fixed"` (fixed-effects, no heterogeneity).

args.name: lang
args.doc: Character. Language for messages: `"pt"` (default), `"en"`, `"es"`.

args.name: verbose
args.doc: Logical. Print progress messages. Default `TRUE`.

name: sus_mod_sensitivity
title: Stratified Climate-Health Sensitivity Analysis from DLNM Fits
description:
Compares exposure-response curves and cumulative relative risks across
population strata (e.g., age groups, sexes, income quintiles, municipalities)
to identify which subgroups are most sensitive to a climate exposure.
Takes a named list of `climasus_dlnm` fits — one per stratum — and extracts
the cumulative RR at user-specified hot and cold percentiles for each,
producing a ranked comparison table and full stratum-specific ERCs for
plotting.

args.name: fits
args.doc:
A named list of `climasus_dlnm` objects, one per stratum.
  All fits must use the same `climate_col` and were produced by
  `sus_mod_dlnm()`. If the list is unnamed, strata are labelled
  `"Stratum 1"`, `"Stratum 2"`, etc.

args.name: hot_percentile
args.doc:
Numeric in (0, 1). Quantile of the observed exposure
  distribution used as the "hot" comparison point. Default: `0.99` (99th
  percentile — extreme heat). Must be greater than `cold_percentile`.

args.name: cold_percentile
args.doc:
Numeric in (0, 1). Quantile of the observed exposure
  distribution used as the "cold" comparison point. Default: `0.01` (1st
  percentile — extreme cold). Must be less than `hot_percentile`.

args.name: stratum_labels
args.doc:
Named character vector or `NULL`. Custom display labels
  for the strata, with names matching `names(fits)`. If `NULL`, the list
  names are used as-is. Useful for plotting (e.g.,
  `c(age_65plus = "65+ years", age_1864 = "18-64 years")`).

args.name: alpha
args.doc:
Numeric. Significance level for confidence intervals.
  Default: `0.05`.

args.name: lang
args.doc:
Character. Language for messages: `"pt"` (default), `"en"`,
  `"es"`.

args.name: verbose
args.doc: Logical. Print progress messages. Default `TRUE`.

name: sus_mod_spacetime_bayes
title: Bayesian Spatiotemporal Hierarchical Model with INLA

args.name: df
args.doc:
A `data.frame` or `climasus_df` at the `aggregate`, `spatial`, or
  `climate` pipeline stage. Must contain `code_muni` (7-digit IBGE code),
  the `outcome` column, the `time_col` column, all columns named in
  `covariates`, and optionally an `offset` column. The data must form a
  panel (one row per area-time combination).

args.name: outcome
args.doc: Character. Name of the response (count) column in `df`.

args.name: W
args.doc:
A `climasus_weights` object produced by
  [sus_mod_spatial_weights()]. Must contain either a `$W` dense matrix or
  a `$nb` neighbour list used to build the INLA graph.

args.name: time_col
args.doc:
Character. Name of the column containing time information.
  Can be a `Date`, `integer`, or `character` column. Default `"date"`.

args.name: time_unit
args.doc:
Character. Temporal aggregation unit used to build the
  integer time index. One of `"year"`, `"month"`, `"week"`, or `"auto"`
  (automatic detection based on date range). Default `"auto"`.

args.name: covariates
args.doc:
Character vector of covariate column names in `df` to
  include as fixed effects. Default `NULL` (intercept-only model).

args.name: offset
args.doc:
Character. Name of a **population or expected counts** column
  in `df` (natural scale). Entered as `log(offset)` in the linear
  predictor. Default `NULL`.

args.name: family
args.doc:
Character. Likelihood family. One of `"poisson"` (default),
  `"nbinomial"` (negative binomial), `"binomial"`, or `"gaussian"`.

args.name: spatial_model
args.doc:
Character. Spatial random effect structure. One of
  `"bym2"` (default), `"bym"`, `"besag"`, or `"iid"`. See **Spatial model
  options**.

args.name: temporal_model
args.doc:
Character. Temporal random effect structure. One of
  `"rw1"` (default), `"rw2"`, `"ar1"`, or `"iid_time"`.

args.name: interaction_type
args.doc:
Character. Space-time interaction type following
  Knorr-Held (2000). One of `"none"` (default), `"I"`, `"II"`, `"III"`,
  or `"IV"`. See **Knorr-Held interaction types**.

args.name: pc_prior_u
args.doc:
Positive numeric. PC prior parameter \eqn{u} for the
  BYM2 spatial precision: \eqn{P(\sigma > u) = \alpha}. Default `0.5`.

args.name: pc_prior_alpha
args.doc:
Numeric in (0, 1). PC prior parameter \eqn{\alpha}
  (tail probability). Default `0.01`.

args.name: compute_waic
args.doc:
Logical. If `TRUE` (default), compute WAIC via
  `control.compute = list(waic = TRUE)`.

args.name: compute_cpo
args.doc:
Logical. If `TRUE`, compute CPO/PIT values via
  `control.compute = list(cpo = TRUE)`. Default `FALSE` (slower).

args.name: exceedance_threshold
args.doc:
Positive numeric. Compute \eqn{P(RR > threshold)}
  for each space-time cell using the posterior marginals. Default `1.0`.

args.name: n_samples
args.doc:
Positive integer. Number of posterior samples drawn via
  `INLA::inla.posterior.sample()` to compute RR credible intervals and
  exceedance probabilities. Default `1000L`.

args.name: seed
args.doc: Integer. Random seed for reproducibility. Default `42L`.

args.name: lang
args.doc:
Character. Language for CLI messages: `"pt"` (default),
  `"en"`, `"es"`.

args.name: verbose
args.doc:
Logical. If `TRUE` (default), emit progress messages via
  **cli**.

name: sus_mod_spacetime_exceedance
title: Posterior Exceedance Probabilities from a Spatiotemporal Bayesian Model

args.name: fit
args.doc:
A `climasus_spacetime_bayes` object produced by
  [sus_mod_spacetime_bayes()]. Must contain a `$rr` data frame with
  columns `code_muni`, `time_idx`, `rr_mean`, `rr_lower95`, `rr_upper95`.

args.name: thresholds
args.doc:
Numeric vector of positive RR thresholds for which
  \eqn{P(RR > t)} is computed. Default `c(1.0, 1.5, 2.0)`.

args.name: aggregate_time
args.doc:
Character or `NULL`. If `"year"` or `"month"`,
  aggregate the temporal dimension before reporting exceedances. Requires
  that `time_idx` be coercible to `Date` (formats `"YYYY-MM-DD"` or
  `"YYYY-MM"`). Default `NULL` (no aggregation).

args.name: municipalities
args.doc:
An `sf` object with at least a `code_muni` column
  and geometry. If provided, geometry is joined to the exceedance table
  so the result can be mapped directly. Requires the **sf** package.
  Default `NULL`.

args.name: lang
args.doc:
Character. Language for CLI messages: `"pt"` (default),
  `"en"`, or `"es"`.

args.name: verbose
args.doc:
Logical. If `TRUE` (default), print progress messages via
  **cli**.

name: sus_mod_spacetime_predict
title: Generate Predictions from a Fitted Space-Time Bayesian Model

args.name: fit
args.doc:
A `climasus_spacetime_bayes` object from
  [sus_mod_spacetime_bayes()].

args.name: newdata
args.doc:
A `data.frame` with at least columns `code_muni` (7-digit
  IBGE code) and `time_idx` (integer period index, 1-based, matching the
  encoding used in the original fit) plus any covariate columns required
  by the model. If `NULL` and `horizon > 0`, a synthetic grid is constructed
  by crossing all fitted municipalities with the next `horizon` time steps,
  using covariate values from the last obse

args.name: horizon
args.doc:
Non-negative integer. Number of periods ahead to predict when
  `newdata` is `NULL`. If `newdata` is provided, `horizon` must be `0L`
  (default).

args.name: covariates_new
args.doc:
Named list of covariate overrides for scenario
  analysis. Each element must be a scalar (applied to all rows) or a vector
  of length `nrow(newdata)`. Applied after `newdata` columns are extracted.
  Default `NULL` (no overrides).

args.name: include_ci
args.doc:
Logical. If `TRUE` (default), compute 95\% approximate
  credible intervals via variance propagation from posterior standard
  deviations in `fit$fixed`, `fit$spatial_re`, and `fit$temporal_re`.

args.name: return_samples
args.doc:
Logical. If `TRUE`, attempt to draw posterior
  predictive samples from the stored INLA fit object (`fit$inla_fit`).
  Requires the model to have been fitted with `return_inla_fit = TRUE` and
  the **INLA** package to be installed. Default `FALSE`.

args.name: lang
args.doc:
Character. Language for CLI messages: `"pt"` (default),
  `"en"`, or `"es"`.

args.name: verbose
args.doc:
Logical. If `TRUE` (default), print progress messages via
  **cli**.

name: sus_mod_spatial_bayes
title: Bayesian CAR / BYM Disease Mapping with Climate Covariates

args.name: df
args.doc:
A `data.frame` or `climasus_df` at the aggregate pipeline stage.
  Must contain `code_muni` (7-digit IBGE code), the `outcome` column, all
  columns named in `covariates`, and optionally an `offset` column.
  Rows are sorted by `code_muni` internally to align with `W`.

args.name: outcome
args.doc:
Character. Name of the response variable column in `df`
  (e.g. `"deaths"`, `"hospitalizations"`).

args.name: W
args.doc:
A `climasus_weights` object produced by
  [sus_mod_spatial_weights()]. Must contain either a `$W` dense matrix or a
  `$nb` neighbour list from which a binary adjacency matrix is built.

args.name: covariates
args.doc:
Character vector of covariate column names in `df` to
  include as fixed effects. Default `NULL` (intercept-only model).

args.name: offset
args.doc:
Character. Name of an **expected counts** column in `df`
  (on the natural scale, not log-transformed). Used as `log(offset)` in the
  linear predictor for Poisson models. Default `NULL`.

args.name: family
args.doc:
Character. Response distribution. One of `"poisson"`
  (default), `"binomial"`, or `"gaussian"`. Note: `"gaussian"` is not
  supported for `model = "bym2"`.

args.name: model
args.doc:
Character. Spatial model structure. One of:
  \itemize{
    \item `"bym"` (default) -- Besag-York-Mollie model via CARBayes/MCMC.
    \item `"leroux"` -- Leroux CAR model via CARBayes/MCMC.
    \item `"independent"` -- Independent random effects via CARBayes/MCMC.
    \item `"bym2"` -- Reparameterised BYM2 model via INLA with penalised
      complexity priors. Requires the **INLA** package (not on

args.name: n_iter
args.doc:
Positive integer. Total number of MCMC iterations
  (including burn-in). Used only for CARBayes models (`"bym"`, `"leroux"`,
  `"independent"`). Default `10000`. Ignored for `model = "bym2"`.

args.name: burnin
args.doc:
Positive integer. Number of initial MCMC iterations to
  discard as burn-in. Must be less than `n_iter`. Used only for CARBayes
  models. Default `2000`. Ignored for `model = "bym2"`.

args.name: thin
args.doc:
Positive integer. Thinning interval. Used only for CARBayes
  models. Default `10`. Ignored for `model = "bym2"`.

args.name: prior_tau2
args.doc:
Numeric vector of length 2. Shape and rate parameters of
  the Inverse-Gamma prior for the variance of spatial random effects.
  Used only for CARBayes models. Default `c(1, 0.01)` (weakly informative).
  Ignored for `model = "bym2"` (which uses PC priors instead).

args.name: seed
args.doc:
Integer. Random seed passed to [base::set.seed()] for
  reproducible MCMC (CARBayes models) or to `INLA::inla.set.control.compute`
  via `inla.seed` (BYM2). Default `42`.

args.name: lang
args.doc:
Character. Language for CLI messages: `"pt"` (default),
  `"en"`, `"es"`.

args.name: verbose
args.doc:
Logical. If `TRUE` (default), print progress messages
  via **cli**.

name: sus_mod_spatial_moran
title: Global Moran's I and LISA Local Autocorrelation for Health Outcomes
description:
Computes Moran's I global spatial autocorrelation statistic and Local
Indicators of Spatial Association (LISA) for a numeric health outcome
measured at municipality level. The global statistic tests whether the
outcome is spatially clustered across the study region; the local statistics
identify significant **High-High** (hotspots), **Low-Low** (coldspots),
**High-Low**, and **Low-High** spatial outlier clusters.

args.name: df
args.doc:
A `data.frame` or `climasus_df` containing at least the columns
  `code_muni` (7-digit IBGE municipality code) and the outcome column
  named by `outcome`. The row order must correspond to the spatial units
  encoded in `W` unless `municipalities` is supplied to subset and reorder.

args.name: outcome
args.doc:
Character. Name of the numeric outcome column in `df`
  (e.g. `"deaths"`, `"rate_100k"`, `"hospitalization_rate"`).

args.name: W
args.doc:
A `climasus_weights` object produced by
  `sus_mod_spatial_weights()`. Must contain a `listw` element (a
  `listw`-class object from **spdep**) whose length matches `nrow(df)` after
  any filtering by `municipalities`.

args.name: municipalities
args.doc:
Optional character or integer vector of `code_muni`
  values to include. When supplied the function subsets and reorders `df`
  to match this vector before computation. Default `NULL` (use all rows).

args.name: permutations
args.doc:
Positive integer. Number of Monte Carlo permutations
  for both global and local inference. Default `999`.

args.name: alpha
args.doc:
Numeric in (0, 1). Significance threshold applied to
  p-adjusted local Moran p-values when assigning quadrant labels.
  Default `0.05`.

args.name: adjust_p
args.doc:
Character. Method passed to [stats::p.adjust()] for
  multiple-testing correction of local Moran p-values. One of `"fdr"`
  (default), `"bonferroni"`, or `"none"`.

args.name: lang
args.doc:
Character. Output language for messages: `"pt"` (default),
  `"en"`, or `"es"`.

args.name: verbose
args.doc:
Logical. If `TRUE` (default), print progress messages via
  **cli**.

name: sus_mod_spatial_reg
title: Spatial Regression (SAR / SEM / SDM / SAC) for Climate-Health Associations
description:
Fits maximum-likelihood spatial regression models that account for spatial
spillovers between geographic units (e.g. municipalities) in climate-health
studies. Four model families are supported: the Spatial Autoregressive Model
(SAR / spatial lag), the Spatial Error Model (SEM), the Spatial Durbin Model
(SDM), and the Spatial Autocorrelation Model (SAC).

args.name: df
args.doc:
A `data.frame` or `climasus_df` containing all variables in
  `formula`. If an `sf` object, geometry is dropped automatically.

args.name: formula
args.doc:
A `formula` specifying the outcome and predictors, e.g.
  `deaths ~ mean_temp + precip + pib_pc`.

args.name: W
args.doc:
A `climasus_weights` object produced by
  [sus_mod_spatial_weights()]. Must contain a `listw` element whose
  length matches `nrow(df)`.

args.name: model
args.doc:
Character. Spatial model family. One of `"lag"` (SAR,
  default), `"error"` (SEM), `"durbin"` (SDM), or `"sac"` (SAC).

args.name: method
args.doc:
Character. Estimation method passed to the underlying
  **spatialreg** function. One of `"eigen"` (default), `"LU"`,
  `"Chebyshev"`, or `"MC"`. `"eigen"` is exact; the others are
  approximations suitable for large datasets.

args.name: zero_policy
args.doc:
Logical. If `TRUE` (default), spatial units with no
  neighbours (islands) are allowed and receive a zero spatial lag/error.
  Passed to all **spatialreg** and **spdep** calls.

args.name: lang
args.doc:
Character. Output language for messages: `"pt"` (default),
  `"en"`, or `"es"`.

args.name: verbose
args.doc:
Logical. If `TRUE` (default), print progress messages via
  **cli**.

name: sus_mod_spatial_scan
title: Kulldorff Circular Scan Statistic for Spatial Cluster Detection
description:
Applies the Kulldorff circular spatial scan statistic to detect geographic
clusters of disease excess. The most-likely cluster and any significant
secondary clusters are identified by Monte Carlo hypothesis testing. The
analysis requires a tabular dataset with case counts and population
denominators at the municipality level, plus an `sf` polygon layer
providing municipality boundaries.

args.name: df
args.doc:
A `data.frame` (or `climasus_df`) containing at minimum:
  a `code_muni` column (character or integer) and the columns named by
  `cases`, `population`, and optionally `expected`.

args.name: cases
args.doc:
Character. Name of the column in `df` with case counts
  (non-negative integer or numeric).

args.name: population
args.doc:
Character. Name of the column in `df` with the
  at-risk population denominator.

args.name: municipalities
args.doc:
An `sf` object with POLYGON or MULTIPOLYGON
  geometry and a `code_muni` column used to join to `df`.

args.name: expected
args.doc:
Character or `NULL`. Name of the column in `df` with
  the expected number of cases under the null model. When `NULL`
  (default), `SpatialEpi::kulldorff()` computes expected counts
  internally from the overall rate.

args.name: max_pop_frac
args.doc:
Numeric in (0, 1]. Maximum fraction of the total
  population that a single cluster window may contain. Default `0.5`.

args.name: n_simulations
args.doc:
Positive integer. Number of Monte Carlo simulations
  for hypothesis testing. Default `999`. Increase to `9999` for
  publication-quality p-values.

args.name: alpha
args.doc:
Numeric in (0, 1). Significance level for secondary cluster
  filtering. Default `0.05`.

args.name: lang
args.doc: Character. Output language: `"pt"` (default), `"en"`, `"es"`.

args.name: verbose
args.doc: Logical. Print progress messages. Default `TRUE`.

name: sus_mod_spatial_weights
title: Build Spatial Weights from Municipality Polygons
description:
Constructs a `spdep` spatial weights object from an `sf` data frame of
municipality (or any polygon) boundaries. The result — a `climasus_weights`
object — is the required input for all other `sus_mod_spatial_*` functions
in the climasus4r pipeline.

Contiguity neighbours are detected with [spdep::poly2nb()], which supports
both Queen (default, shared edge **or** vertex) and Rook (shared edge only)
contiguity. Municipalities that share no boundary ("spatial islands") are
automatically detected and reported. The resulting neighbour list is
normalised to a `listw` object via [spdep::nb2listw()].

args.name: municipalities
args.doc:
An `sf` object with **polygon** or **multipolygon**
  geometry. Typically the result of [geobr::read_municipality()] or any
  shape file read with [sf::st_read()]. A column named `code_muni` is used
  as row labels when present; otherwise integer indices are used.

args.name: style
args.doc:
Character. Weight normalisation style passed to
  [spdep::nb2listw()]. One of `"W"` (default), `"B"`, `"C"`, `"U"`,
  `"S"`, `"minmax"`. See **Weight styles** section.

args.name: queen
args.doc:
Logical. If `TRUE` (default) Queen contiguity is used (shared
  edge or vertex). If `FALSE`, Rook contiguity (shared edge only).

args.name: snap
args.doc:
Numeric or `NULL`. Snap distance tolerance passed to
  [spdep::poly2nb()]. `NULL` (default) uses `1e-3`. See **Snap distance**
  section.

args.name: zero_policy
args.doc:
Logical. If `TRUE` (default) municipalities with no
  neighbours ("islands") are allowed — their spatial lag is set to `NA`.
  If `FALSE` an error is raised when islands are found.

args.name: return_matrix
args.doc:
Logical. If `TRUE`, a dense \eqn{n \times n} spatial
  weights matrix `W` is included in the output (slot `$W`). This can be
  memory-intensive for large datasets. Default `FALSE`.

args.name: lang
args.doc:
Character. Language for CLI messages: `"pt"` (default),
  `"en"`, `"es"`.

args.name: verbose
args.doc: Logical. Print progress messages. Default `TRUE`.

name: sus_mod_swot
title: SWOT Analysis for Climate-Health Surveillance
description:
Synthesizes pre-computed climasus model objects into a Strengths,
Weaknesses, Opportunities, and Threats (SWOT) framework for climate-health
risk communication and territorial planning. Each quadrant aggregates
normalized indicators (0–100 scale) extracted from the supplied model objects;
quadrant scores are returned both as continuous values and as user-defined
categorical labels.

args.name: vulnerability
args.doc:
A `climasus_vi` object from
  [sus_mod_vulnerability_index()], or `NULL`. Provides per-city VI scores,
  exposure, sensitivity, and adaptive capacity indicators.

args.name: af
args.doc:
A `climasus_af` object from [sus_mod_af()], or `NULL`. Provides
  total, heat, and cold attributable fraction percentages.

args.name: burden
args.doc:
A `climasus_burden` object from [sus_mod_burden()], or `NULL`.
  Provides city-level ranked attributable numbers and fractions.

args.name: dlnm
args.doc:
A `climasus_dlnm` object from [sus_mod_dlnm()], or `NULL`.
  Provides the heat exposure-response RR at the 95th percentile of
  temperature.

args.name: sensitivity
args.doc:
A `climasus_sensitivity` object from
  [sus_mod_sensitivity()], or `NULL`. Provides stratum-level RR inequality
  and the maximum hot RR across strata.

args.name: score_type
args.doc:
Character. Which score types to compute:
  `"numeric"` (0–100), `"categorical"` (cut-point labels), or
  `"both"` (default).

args.name: breaks
args.doc:
Numeric vector. Strictly increasing cut-points in (0, 100)
  used to convert numeric scores into categories. Default `c(33, 66)`
  produces three categories. Generates `length(breaks) + 1` categories.

args.name: labels
args.doc:
Character vector or `NULL`. Category labels with length
  `length(breaks) + 1`. `NULL` (default) uses language-appropriate defaults:
  Portuguese `c("Baixo", "Médio", "Alto")`, English `c("Low", "Medium",
  "High")`, Spanish `c("Bajo", "Medio", "Alto")`.

args.name: city_col
args.doc:
Character. Column name of the city/entity identifier in the
  `vulnerability$vi_table`. Defaults to `"city"`. Overridden by
  `vulnerability$meta$city_col` when available.

args.name: lang
args.doc: Character. Language for labels: `"pt"` (default), `"en"`, `"es"`.

args.name: verbose
args.doc: Logical. Print progress messages. Default `TRUE`.

name: sus_mod_vulnerability_index
title: IPCC 3-Pillar Composite Vulnerability Index for Climate-Health Analysis
description:
Builds a city-level composite vulnerability index following the IPCC AR6
vulnerability framework: Vulnerability = f(**Exposure** + **Sensitivity** -
**Adaptive Capacity**). Each pillar is constructed from a user-supplied
data frame of city-level numeric indicators. Indicators are normalized
(min-max or z-score), weighted within pillars, and combined into a final
score ∈ \[0, 1\] where **1 = most vulnerable**.

**Exposure** quantifies how much climate hazard a city experiences (e.g.,
mean maximum temperature, number of heat-wave days, threshold exceedances
from [sus_climate_aggregate()]). **Sen

args.name: exposure_df
args.doc:
A data frame with one row per city and numeric columns
  representing **exposure** indicators (higher value = more hazard).
  Typically derived by summarising a `climasus_df` produced by
  [sus_climate_aggregate()]. Must contain the column named `city_col`.

args.name: sensitivity_df
args.doc:
A data frame with numeric **sensitivity** indicators
  (higher value = more sensitive population). Can include demographic
  columns (% elderly, % children), health-outcome rates, or any other
  stratum-level vulnerability marker. If `fits` is also provided, the
  DLNM-derived metrics are merged into this data frame.

args.name: adaptive_capacity_df
args.doc:
A data frame with numeric **adaptive capacity**
  indicators (higher value = *more* adaptive capacity = *less* vulnerable).
  This pillar is inverted internally before computing VI. Typical columns
  come from [sus_census_join()] (e.g., HDI, literacy rate, piped-water
  coverage) or [sus_spatial_join()] (e.g., hospital density per capita).

args.name: fits
args.doc:
Optional named list of `climasus_dlnm` objects (one per city,
  names = city identifiers). When provided, the function extracts
  `dlnm_hot_rr` (cumulative RR at `hot_percentile`), `dlnm_cold_rr`
  (cumulative RR at `cold_percentile`), and `dlnm_sensitivity_index` for
  each city and merges them into the sensitivity pillar.

args.name: city_col
args.doc:
Character. Name of the city identifier column present in
  every supplied data frame. Default `"city"`.

args.name: normalize
args.doc:
Character. Normalization method: `"minmax"` (default,
  maps each indicator to \[0, 1\]) or `"zscore"` (standardizes by mean and
  SD — useful for heterogeneous indicator scales but requires back-
  transformation for interpretation).

args.name: weights
args.doc:
Named numeric vector. Within-pillar indicator weights. Names
  must match indicator column names in the respective data frame. Indicators
  not listed receive weight 1. Weights are normalized to sum to 1 within
  each pillar. Example: `c(pct_elderly = 2, pct_children = 1)` doubles the
  influence of elderly share in the sensitivity pillar.

args.name: pillar_weights
args.doc:
Named numeric vector with elements `exposure`,
  `sensitivity`, and `adaptive_capacity`. Controls the relative contribution
  of each pillar to the final VI. Default `c(1, 1, 1)` (equal weights).
  Absent pillars automatically receive weight 0.

args.name: hot_percentile
args.doc:
Numeric in (0, 1). Quantile used as the "hot"
  threshold for DLNM extraction. Default `0.99`. Only used when `fits` is
  provided.

args.name: cold_percentile
args.doc:
Numeric in (0, 1). Quantile used as the "cold"
  threshold for DLNM extraction. Default `0.01`. Only used when `fits` is
  provided.

args.name: lang
args.doc:
Character. Language for messages: `"pt"` (default), `"en"`,
  `"es"`.

args.name: verbose
args.doc: Logical. Print progress messages. Default `TRUE`.

name: sus_rap_addin_export
title: RStudio Addin: Export Selected Pipeline as RAP
description:
Captures the selected code in the active RStudio source editor, parses it
as a `climasus4r` pipeline, and exports it as a RAP script via
[sus_rap_export()]. The output file is placed in the same directory as the
active source file.

Registered in `inst/rstudio/addins.dcf` as an interactive addin.

name: sus_rap_export
title: Export a climasus4r Pipeline as a Reproducible Analytical Pipeline (RAP)
description:
Converts a `climasus4r` pipeline expression into a portable, self-contained
artefact  - an R script (`.R`), RMarkdown document (`.Rmd`), Quarto document
(`.qmd`), or an encapsulated R function  - suitable for sharing, archiving,
and re-execution. The exported file embeds session metadata (R version,
package versions, timestamp, platform), an editable parameters block, and
optional validation checks, following Reproducible Analytical Pipeline
principles.

args.name: pipeline
args.doc:
A pipeline expression (created with `quote()` or
  `rlang::quo()`) containing `sus_*` function calls connected by `|>` or
  `%>%`. Pass `NULL` to capture from the calling context.

args.name: file_path
args.doc:
`character(1)`  - Output file path. Extension informs format
  detection. Pass `NULL` to return content without writing to disk.

args.name: format
args.doc:
`character(1)`  - One of `"script"`, `"rmarkdown"`,
  `"quarto"`, or `"function"`. Inferred from `file_path` extension when
  possible.

args.name: include_metadata
args.doc:
`logical(1)`  - Embed R version, package versions,
  timestamp, and platform. Default `TRUE`.

args.name: include_validation
args.doc:
`logical(1)`  - Add a validation block (`stopifnot`
  + NA-rate check). Default `TRUE`.

args.name: include_documentation
args.doc:
`character(1)`  - Comment density in the
  exported code: `"minimal"`, `"standard"` (default), or `"comprehensive"`.

args.name: output_type
args.doc:
`character(1)`  - Purpose of the document:
  `"analysis"`, `"dashboard"`, or `"report"`. Affects visualisation
  sections in Rmd/Quarto outputs.

args.name: lang
args.doc:
`character(1)`  - Language for comments and messages: `"pt"`
  (default), `"en"`, or `"es"`.

args.name: overwrite
args.doc: `logical(1)`  - Overwrite an existing file. Default `FALSE`.

name: sus_rap_from_recipe
title: Import and Optionally Run a RAP Recipe
description:
Reads a YAML recipe exported by [sus_rap_recipe()] and reconstructs a
`rap_object`. Parameter overrides can be supplied at import time. Passing
`execute = TRUE` immediately re-runs the pipeline using [sus_rap_run()].

args.name: recipe_path
args.doc: `character(1)`  - Path to the `.yaml` recipe file.

args.name: override_params
args.doc:
Named list of parameters to override from the recipe
  (e.g. `list(uf = "RJ", years = 2022:2024)`).

args.name: execute
args.doc:
`logical(1)`  - Run the pipeline immediately after loading.
  Default `FALSE`.

args.name: lang
args.doc: `character(1)`  - Language for messages.

name: sus_rap_gui
title: Launch an Interactive GUI for Editing and Running RAPs
description:
Opens a Shiny application that lets users visually configure pipeline
parameters (UF, years, system, aggregation), preview the generated code,
and execute or export the RAP  - all without writing R code directly.

args.name: rap
args.doc:
A `rap_object` (from [sus_rap_read()]) to pre-populate the
  interface. Pass `NULL` (default) to start from a blank template.

args.name: launch.browser
args.doc:
`logical(1)`  - Open in the system browser.
  Default `TRUE`.

name: sus_rap_inspect
title: Inspect or Diff rap_object(s)
description:
Prints a structured summary of a `rap_object`. When two objects are
supplied, performs a side-by-side diff showing changed parameters, added or
removed pipeline steps, and package version drift.

args.name: rap
args.doc: A `rap_object` returned by [sus_rap_read()].

args.name: rap2
args.doc:
A second `rap_object` for comparison, or `NULL` (default) for
  single-object summary only.

args.name: verbose
args.doc: `logical(1)`  - Show detailed step list. Default `TRUE`.

args.name: lang
args.doc: `character(1)`  - Language for messages.

name: sus_rap_make
title: Execute a targets Pipeline from a RAP
description:
Runs a `_targets.R` pipeline (generated by [sus_rap_targets()]) using
`targets::tar_make()`, with optional parallelism via `workers`.

args.name: rap
args.doc:
A `rap_object`, a `tar_rap_object`, or a `character(1)` path to
  a `_targets.R` file. When a `rap_object` is passed, a temporary
  `_targets.R` is generated automatically.

args.name: workers
args.doc: `integer(1)`  - Parallel workers. Default `1`.

args.name: reporter
args.doc:
`character(1)`  - Progress reporter for `tar_make()`.
  Default `"verbose"`.

args.name: branching
args.doc:
`character(1)`  - Branching strategy when `rap` is a
  `rap_object` (ignored when a file path is passed). Default `"none"`.

args.name: lang
args.doc: `character(1)`  - Language for messages.

name: sus_rap_read
title: Read an Exported RAP File
description:
Reads a file produced by [sus_rap_export()] and reconstructs a live
`rap_object` containing session metadata, configurable parameters, and the
pipeline step list. The result can be inspected with [sus_rap_inspect()],
re-executed with [sus_rap_run()], or patched with [sus_rap_update()].

args.name: file_path
args.doc:
`character(1)`  - Path to an `.R`, `.Rmd`, or `.qmd` file
  exported by [sus_rap_export()].

args.name: lang
args.doc:
`character(1)`  - Language for messages: `"pt"` (default),
  `"en"`, or `"es"`.

args.name: validate
args.doc: `logical(1)`  - Check structural integrity. Default `TRUE`.

name: sus_rap_recipe
title: Export a RAP as a Compact YAML Recipe
description:
Serialises the essential information of a `rap_object`  - parameters,
pipeline steps, and package versions  - into a compact YAML file (typically
under 50 lines). The recipe can be shared with colleagues and re-imported
with [sus_rap_from_recipe()].

args.name: rap
args.doc: A `rap_object` returned by [sus_rap_read()] or [sus_rap_export()].

args.name: file_path
args.doc:
`character(1)`  - Output path for the `.yaml` file. If
  `NULL`, a timestamped file is created in the working directory.

args.name: include_data_hash
args.doc:
`logical(1)`  - Compute and embed a hash of the
  input parameters for traceability. Default `TRUE`.

args.name: lang
args.doc: `character(1)`  - Language for messages.

args.name: overwrite
args.doc: `logical(1)`  - Overwrite existing file. Default `FALSE`.

name: sus_rap_run
title: Re-execute an Exported RAP
description:
Reconstructs and executes the pipeline contained in a `rap_object`. Any
parameters passed via `...` override the originals before execution, making
it easy to replay an analysis for a different state or year range.

args.name: rap
args.doc: A `rap_object` returned by [sus_rap_read()].

args.name: envir
args.doc: `environment`  - Execution environment. Default `parent.frame()`.

args.name: dry_run
args.doc:
`logical(1)`  - Print the reconstructed code without executing.
  Default `FALSE`.

args.name: lang
args.doc: `character(1)`  - Language for messages.

name: sus_rap_targets
title: Generate a targets Pipeline from a climasus4r RAP
description:
Converts a `rap_object` or a pipeline expression into a ready-to-use
`_targets.R` file. The generated script uses `targets::tar_target()` with
optional dynamic branching over UFs, years, or their Cartesian product.

args.name: pipeline
args.doc:
A `rap_object` (from [sus_rap_read()]) or a quoted
  pipeline expression (from `quote()`).

args.name: file_path
args.doc: `character(1)`  - Output path. Default `"_targets.R"`.

args.name: branching
args.doc:
`character(1)`  - Branching strategy: `"none"` (default),
  `"uf"`, `"year"`, or `"cross"`.

args.name: workers
args.doc: `integer(1)`  - Number of parallel workers. Default `1`.

args.name: include_qc
args.doc: `logical(1)`  - Add a quality-control target. Default `TRUE`.

args.name: lang
args.doc: `character(1)`  - Language for messages.

args.name: overwrite
args.doc: `logical(1)`  - Overwrite existing file. Default `FALSE`.

name: sus_rap_template
title: Scaffold a Reproducible Analytical Pipeline Project
description:
Creates a ready-to-use project directory with a `_targets.R` pipeline,
a parameterised Quarto report, optional `renv` dependency snapshot,
optional GitHub Actions CI workflow, and a structured file layout
following RAP best practices.

args.name: path
args.doc:
`character(1)`  - Parent directory where `<name>/` will be
  created (e.g. `"~/projetos"`).

args.name: name
args.doc: `character(1)`  - Project name; becomes the directory name.

args.name: system
args.doc:
`character(1)`  - DATASUS system for the template pipeline
  (e.g. `"SIM-DO"`, `"SINAN"`, `"SIH"`). Default `"SIM-DO"`.

args.name: include_renv
args.doc:
`logical(1)`  - Initialise `renv` for dependency
  management. Requires the `renv` package. Default `TRUE`.

args.name: include_targets
args.doc:
`logical(1)`  - Create `_targets.R` and `run.R`.
  Default `TRUE`.

args.name: include_quarto
args.doc: `logical(1)`  - Create `analysis.qmd`. Default `TRUE`.

args.name: include_github_actions
args.doc:
`logical(1)`  - Create
  `.github/workflows/rap.yml`. Default `FALSE`.

args.name: lang
args.doc:
`character(1)`  - Language for generated comments and messages:
  `"pt"` (default), `"en"`, or `"es"`.

name: sus_rap_update
title: Update Parameters in an Exported RAP File
description:
Applies targeted patches to the editable `params <- list(...)` block of an
exported RAP file without re-exporting from scratch. A `.bak` backup is
created before any modification.

args.name: rap
args.doc: A `rap_object` returned by [sus_rap_read()].

args.name: backup
args.doc:
`logical(1)`  - Create a `.bak` backup before writing.
  Default `TRUE`.

args.name: lang
args.doc: `character(1)`  - Language for messages.

name: sus_socio_compute_indicators
title: Compute Socioeconomic and Epidemiological Indicators
description:
Computes a set of standardised indicators from the columns already present
in a `climasus_df` object (typically the output of [sus_census_join()]).
Indicators span demographics, socioeconomic vulnerability, mortality,
morbidity, maternal-child health, and health-resource availability.
Column names from the census aggregation step (e.g. `V003_sum`) can be
mapped to the formula-space names expected by each indicator via
`col_mapping`.

args.name: df
args.doc:
A `climasus_df` object at stage `>= "spatial"`. Typically the
  output of [sus_census_join()].

args.name: indicators
args.doc:
Character vector of indicator IDs to compute. If `NULL`
  (default), all indicators whose required columns are present in `df`
  are computed automatically.  Use [sus_socio_list_indicators()] to
  inspect the full catalogue.

args.name: col_mapping
args.doc:
Named list mapping formula-space variable names to actual
  column names in `df`.  For example:
  ```r
  list(pop_young = "pop_0_14_sum", total_hh = "V003_sum")
  ```
  Only mappings that override the defaults need to be specified.

args.name: confidence_level
args.doc:
Numeric. Confidence level for Poisson / Binomial
  intervals.  Default `0.95`.

args.name: add_ci
args.doc:
Logical. If `TRUE` (default), adds `*_low` and `*_high`
  columns for indicators that support uncertainty quantification
  (Poisson for rates, Wilson score for proportions).

args.name: lang
args.doc: Language for messages: `"pt"` (default), `"en"`, `"es"`.

args.name: verbose
args.doc: Logical. Print progress messages.  Default `TRUE`.

name: sus_socio_list_indicators
title: List Available Indicators in the Catalogue
description:
Returns a data frame describing all indicators available in
`sus_socio_compute_indicators()`, including their IDs, names,
categories, required columns, and uncertainty method.

args.name: lang
args.doc: Language for indicator names: `"pt"` (default), `"en"`, `"es"`.

args.name: category
args.doc:
Optional character vector to filter by category. Use `NULL`
  (default) to return all.
